rules.raw_text: Where none of the other supervisory authorities concerned has objected to the draft decision submitted by the lead supervisory authority within the period referred to in paragraphs 4 and 5, the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall be deemed to be in agreement with that draft decision and shall be bound by it.

rules.action: adopt and notify decision
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority shall adopt and notify the decision to the main establishment or single establishment of the controller or processor, as the case may be and inform the other supervisory authorities concerned and the Board of the decision in question, including a summary of the relevant facts and grounds.

rules.entity: supervisory authority with which a complaint has been lodged
rules.action: inform complainant on decision
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authority with which a complaint has been lodged shall inform the complainant on the decision.

rules.entity: supervisory authority with which complaint was lodged
rules.action: adopt decision, notify it to complainant
rules.condition: By derogation from paragraph 7, where a complaint is dismissed or rejected
rules.raw_text: By derogation from paragraph 7, where a complaint is dismissed or rejected, the supervisory authority with which the complaint was lodged shall adopt the decision and notify it to the complainant.

rules.entity: supervisory authority with which complaint was lodged
rules.action: inform controller thereof
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authority with which the complaint was lodged shall inform the controller thereof.

rules.entity: lead supervisory authority, supervisory authorities concerned
rules.action: agree to dismiss or reject parts of a complaint and to act on other parts of that complaint
rules.raw_text: Where the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned agree to dismiss or reject parts of a complaint and to act on other parts of that complaint, a separate decision shall be adopted.

rules.action: provide relevant information and mutual assistance
rules.condition: for consistent implementation and application of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: Supervisory authorities shall provide each other with relevant information and mutual assistance in order to implement and apply this Regulation in a consistent manner, and shall put in place measures for effective cooperation with one another.

rules.action: execute supervisory measures such as requests to carry out prior authorisations and consultations, inspections and investigations
rules.condition: for mutual assistance
rules.raw_text: Mutual assistance shall cover, in particular, information requests and supervisory measures, such as requests to carry out prior authorisations and consultations, inspections and investigations.

rules.action: take appropriate measures to reply to request
rules.condition: without undue delay and no later than one month after receiving the request
rules.raw_text: Each supervisory authority shall take all appropriate measures required to reply to a request of another supervisory authority without undue delay and no later than one month after receiving the request.

rules.action: transmit relevant information on the conduct of an investigation
rules.raw_text: Such measures may include, in particular, the transmission of relevant information on the conduct of an investigation.

rules.action: contain necessary information, including purpose and reasons
rules.raw_text: Requests for assistance shall contain all the necessary information, including the purpose of and reasons for the request.

rules.action: be used only for the purpose for which it was requested
rules.raw_text: Information exchanged shall be used only for the purpose for which it was requested.

rules.action: not allowed unless competence or compliance infringement
rules.raw_text: The requested supervisory authority shall not refuse to comply with the request unless: it is not competent for the subject-matter of the request or for the measures it is requested to execute; or compliance with the request would infringe this Regulation or Union or Member State law to which the supervisory authority receiving the request is subject.

rules.action: inform requesting supervisory authority of results or progress
rules.scope: of the measures taken to respond to the request
rules.raw_text: The requested supervisory authority shall inform the requesting supervisory authority of the results or, as the case may be, of the progress of the measures taken in order to respond to the request.

rules.action: provide reasons for any refusal to comply with a request pursuant to paragraph 4
rules.raw_text: The requested supervisory authority shall provide reasons for any refusal to comply with a request pursuant to paragraph 4.

rules.action: supply information by electronic means, using a standardised format
rules.raw_text: Requested supervisory authorities shall, as a rule, supply the information requested by other supervisory authorities by electronic means, using a standardised format.

rules.action: not charge a fee for any action taken
rules.condition: pursuant to a request for mutual assistance
rules.raw_text: Requested supervisory authorities shall not charge a fee for any action taken by them pursuant to a request for mutual assistance.

rules.action: agree on rules to indemnify each other for specific expenditure
rules.condition: in exceptional circumstances
rules.raw_text: Supervisory authorities may agree on rules to indemnify each other for specific expenditure arising from the provision of mutual assistance in exceptional circumstances.

rules.action: adopt provisional measure on the territory of its Member State
rules.condition: if information referred to in paragraph 5 is not provided within one month of receiving the request
rules.raw_text: Where a supervisory authority does not provide the information referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article within one month of receiving the request of another supervisory authority, the requesting supervisory authority may adopt a provisional measure on the territory of its Member State in accordance with Article 55(1).

rules.action: require urgent binding decision from the Board pursuant to Article 66(2)
rules.condition: if urgent need to act under Article 66(1) is presumed to be met
rules.raw_text: In that case, the urgent need to act under Article 66(1) shall be presumed to be met and require an urgent binding decision from the Board pursuant to Article 66(2).

rules.action: conduct joint operations and investigations, joint enforcement measures
rules.condition: appropriate
rules.scope: involvement of members or staff of supervisory authorities from other Member States
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authorities shall, where appropriate, conduct joint operations including joint investigations and joint enforcement measures in which members or staff of the supervisory authorities of other Member States are involved.

rules.action: participate in joint operations
rules.condition: controller or processor has establishments in several Member States or significant number of data subjects are likely to be substantially affected by processing operations
rules.scope: supervisory authority of each of those Member States
rules.raw_text: Where the controller or processor has establishments in several Member States or where a significant number of data subjects in more than one Member State are likely to be substantially affected by processing operations, a supervisory authority of each of those Member States shall have the right to participate in joint operations.

rules.entity: supervisory authority competent pursuant to Article 56 (1) or (4)
rules.action: invite the supervisory authority of each of those Member States to take part in joint operations, respond without delay to the request of a supervisory authority to participate
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 56 (1) or (4) shall invite the supervisory authority of each of those Member States to take part in the joint operations and shall respond without delay to the request of a supervisory authority to participate.

rules.action: confer powers, including investigative powers
rules.condition: in accordance with Member State law and with the seconding supervisory authority’s authorisation
rules.scope: seconding supervisory authority’s members or staff involved in joint operations
rules.raw_text: A supervisory authority may, in accordance with Member State law, and with the seconding supervisory authority’s authorisation, confer powers, including investigative powers on the seconding supervisory authority’s members or staff involved in joint operations or, in so far as the law of the Member State of the host supervisory authority permits, allow the seconding supervisory authority’s members or staff to exercise their investigative powers in accordance with the law of the Member State of the seconding supervisory authority.

rules.action: exercised only under guidance and in presence of members or staff of the host supervisory authority
rules.scope: seconding supervisory authority’s members or staff involved in joint operations
rules.raw_text: Such investigative powers may be exercised only under the guidance and in the presence of members or staff of the host supervisory authority.

rules.entity: seconding supervisory authority’s members or staff
rules.action: subject to Member State law of the host supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: The seconding supervisory authority’s members or staff shall be subject to the Member State law of the host supervisory authority.

rules.action: assume responsibility for actions, including liability, of seconding supervisory authority’s staff for any damage caused during their operations
rules.scope: in accordance with the law of the Member State in whose territory they are operating
rules.raw_text: Where, in accordance with paragraph 1, staff of a seconding supervisory authority operate in another Member State, the Member State of the host supervisory authority shall assume responsibility for their actions, including liability, for any damage caused by them during their operations, in accordance with the law of the Member State in whose territory they are operating.

rules.action: make good damage under the conditions applicable to damage caused by its own staff
rules.condition: damage was caused in its territory
rules.raw_text: The Member State in whose territory the damage was caused shall make good such damage under the conditions applicable to damage caused by its own staff.

rules.entity: Member State of the seconding supervisory authority
rules.action: reimburse that other Member State in full any sums it has paid to the persons entitled on their behalf
rules.condition: damage caused by its staff to any person in the territory of another Member State
rules.raw_text: The Member State of the seconding supervisory authority whose staff has caused damage to any person in the territory of another Member State shall reimburse that other Member State in full any sums it has paid to the persons entitled on their behalf.

rules.action: refrain from requesting reimbursement from another Member State in relation to damage referred to in paragraph 4
rules.condition: without prejudice to the exercise of its rights vis-à-vis third parties and with the exception of paragraph 5
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to the exercise of its rights vis-à-vis third parties and with the exception of paragraph 5, each Member State shall refrain, in the case provided for in paragraph 1, from requesting reimbursement from another Member State in relation to damage referred to in paragraph 4.

rules.action: cooperate with each other and, where relevant, with the Commission
rules.condition: through the consistency mechanism as set out in this Section
rules.scope: In order to contribute to the consistent application of this Regulation throughout the Union
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authorities shall cooperate with each other and, where relevant, with the Commission, through the consistency mechanism as set out in this Section.

rules.action: Issue an opinion
rules.condition: Intends to adopt any of the measures listed
rules.raw_text: The Board shall issue an opinion where a competent supervisory authority intends to adopt any of the measures below.

rules.entity: Supervisory authorities, Chair of the Board, Commission
rules.action: Request examination by the Board
rules.condition: For matters of general application or producing effects in more than one Member State, if a competent supervisory authority does not comply with obligations for mutual assistance or joint operations
rules.scope: Regarding matters of general application or producing effects in more than one Member State
rules.raw_text: Any supervisory authority, the Chair of the Board or the Commission may request that any matter of general application or producing effects in more than one Member State be examined by the Board with a view to obtaining an opinion,

rules.action: Issue an opinion
rules.condition: On the matter submitted to it, provided that it has not already issued an opinion on the same matter
rules.scope: Regarding matters referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.raw_text: In the cases referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, the Board shall issue an opinion on the matter submitted to it provided that it has not already issued an opinion on the same matter.

rules.action: Adopt opinion
rules.condition: Within eight weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board
rules.scope: Regarding opinions referred to in paragraph 3
rules.raw_text: That opinion shall be adopted within eight weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board.

rules.action: Extend period for adopting opinion
rules.condition: If necessary due to complexity of subject matter
rules.scope: Regarding opinions referred to in paragraph 3
rules.raw_text: That period may be extended by a further six weeks, taking into account the complexity of the subject matter.

rules.action: Not object
rules.condition: To draft decision referred to in paragraph 1 within a reasonable period indicated by the Chair
rules.scope: Regarding draft decisions referred to in paragraph 1
rules.raw_text: Regarding the draft decision referred to in paragraph 1 circulated to the members of the Board in accordance with paragraph 5, a member which has not objected within a reasonable period indicated by the Chair, shall be deemed to be in agreement with the draft decision.

rules.action: Communicate relevant information to the Board
rules.condition: Without undue delay using a standardized format
rules.raw_text: Supervisory authorities and the Commission shall, without undue delay, communicate by electronic means to the Board, using a standardised format any relevant information,

rules.action: Inform members and Commission
rules.condition: Relevant information communicated to it using a standardized format
rules.scope: Regarding relevant information referred to in rule 7
rules.raw_text: The Chair of the Board shall, without undue, delay inform by electronic means: the members of the Board and the Commission of any relevant information which has been communicated to it using a standardised format.

rules.action: Provide translations
rules.condition: Where necessary
rules.scope: Regarding relevant information referred to in rule 8
rules.raw_text: The secretariat of the Board shall, where necessary, provide translations of relevant information;

rules.action: Inform opinion and make public
rules.condition: Regarding opinion referred to in rule 3
rules.scope: Regarding opinions referred to in paragraph 3
rules.raw_text: the supervisory authority referred to, as the case may be, in paragraphs 1 and 2, and the Commission of the opinion and make it public.

rules.action: adopt a binding decision
rules.condition: In the cases where: 1) a supervisory authority concerned has raised a relevant and reasoned objection to a draft decision of the lead supervisory authority and the lead supervisory authority has not followed the objection or has rejected such an objection as being not relevant or reasoned; 2) there are conflicting views on which of the supervisory authorities concerned is competent for the main establishment; 3) a competent supervisory authority does not request the opinion of the Board in the cases referred to in Article 64 (1), or does not follow the opinion of the Board issued under Article 64.
rules.scope: Concerns all the matters which are the subject of the relevant and reasoned objection, in particular whether there is an infringement of this Regulation.

rules.action: adopt its decision within two weeks following the expiration of the second month referred to in paragraph 2 by a simple majority of the members of the Board.
rules.condition: When the Board has been unable to adopt a decision within the periods referred to in paragraph 2.
rules.raw_text: Where the Board has been unable to adopt a decision within the periods referred to in paragraph 2, it shall adopt its decision within two weeks following the expiration of the second month referred to in paragraph 2 by a simple majority of the members of the Board.

rules.action: adopt the decision by its vote.
rules.condition: When the members of the Board are split.
rules.raw_text: Where the members of the Board are split, the decision shall by adopted by the vote of its Chair.

rules.action: not adopt a decision on the subject matter submitted to the Board under paragraph 1 during the periods referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3.
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authorities concerned shall not adopt a decision on the subject matter submitted to the Board under paragraph 1 during the periods referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3.

rules.action: notify the decision
rules.raw_text: The Chair of the Board shall notify, without undue delay, the decision referred to in paragraph 1 to the supervisory authorities concerned.

rules.action: inform the Commission
rules.raw_text: It shall inform the Commission thereof.

rules.action: publish the decision
rules.raw_text: The decision shall be published on the website of the Board without delay after the supervisory authority has notified the final decision referred to in paragraph 6.

rules.entity: Lead supervisory authority or, as the case may be, the supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged
rules.action: adopt its final decision on the basis of the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, without undue delay and at the latest by one month after the Board has notified its decision.
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority or, as the case may be, the supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged shall adopt its final decision on the basis of the decision referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, without undue delay and at the latest by one month after the Board has notified its decision.

rules.action: adopt provisional measures
rules.condition: exceptional circumstances, urgent need to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects
rules.scope: its own territory with a specified period of validity which shall not exceed three months
rules.raw_text: In exceptional circumstances, where a supervisory authority concerned considers that there is an urgent need to act in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects, it may, by way of derogation from the consistency mechanism referred to in Articles 63, 64 and 65 or the procedure referred to in Article 60, immediately adopt provisional measures intended to produce legal effects on its own territory with a specified period of validity which shall not exceed three months.

rules.action: communicate measures and reasons for adopting them
rules.scope: other supervisory authorities concerned, the Board, and the Commission
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate those measures and the reasons for adopting them to the other supervisory authorities concerned, to the Board and to the Commission.

rules.action: request urgent opinion or binding decision from the Board
rules.condition: considers that final measures need urgently be adopted
rules.raw_text: Where a supervisory authority has taken a measure pursuant to paragraph 1 and considers that final measures need urgently be adopted, it may request an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision from the Board, giving reasons for requesting such opinion or decision.

rules.action: request urgent opinion or binding decision from the Board
rules.condition: competent supervisory authority has not taken an appropriate measure in a situation where there is an urgent need to act, in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects
rules.raw_text: Any supervisory authority may request an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision, as the case may be, from the Board where a competent supervisory authority has not taken an appropriate measure in a situation where there is an urgent need to act, in order to protect the rights and freedoms of data subjects, giving reasons for requesting such opinion or decision.

rules.action: adopt urgent opinion or binding decision within two weeks by simple majority of the members
rules.raw_text: By derogation from Article 64 (3) and Article 65 (2), an urgent opinion or an urgent binding decision referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 of this Article shall be adopted within two weeks by simple majority of the members of the Board.

rules.action: adopt implementing acts of general scope
rules.condition: to specify the arrangements for the exchange of information by electronic means between supervisory authorities, and between supervisory authorities and the Board, in particular the standardised format referred to in Article 64.
rules.raw_text: The Commission may adopt implementing acts of general scope in order to specify the arrangements for the exchange of information by electronic means between supervisory authorities, and between supervisory authorities and the Board, in particular the standardised format referred to in Article 64.

rules.action: is established as a body of the Union and shall have legal personality
rules.raw_text: The European Data Protection Board (the ‘Board’) is hereby established as a body of the Union and shall have legal personality.

rules.action: shall be represented by its Chair
rules.raw_text: The Board shall be represented by its Chair.

rules.action: shall be composed of the head of one supervisory authority of each Member State and of the European Data Protection Supervisor, or their respective representatives
rules.raw_text: The Board shall be composed of the head of one supervisory authority of each Member State and of the European Data Protection Supervisor, or their respective representatives.

rules.entity: In a Member State with more than one supervisory authority responsible for monitoring the application of the provisions pursuant to this Regulation
rules.action: a joint representative shall be appointed in accordance with that Member State’s law
rules.raw_text: Where in a Member State more than one supervisory authority is responsible for monitoring the application of the provisions pursuant to this Regulation, a joint representative shall be appointed in accordance with that Member State’s law.

rules.action: shall have the right to participate in the activities and meetings of the Board without voting right
rules.raw_text: 1 The Commission shall have the right to participate in the activities and meetings of the Board without voting right.

rules.action: shall designate a representative
rules.raw_text: 2 The Commission shall designate a representative.

rules.action: shall communicate to the Commission the activities of the Board
rules.raw_text: 3 The Chair of the Board shall communicate to the Commission the activities of the Board.

rules.action: shall have voting rights only on decisions which concern principles and rules applicable to the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies which correspond in substance to those of this Regulation
rules.condition: In the cases referred to in Article 65
rules.raw_text: In the cases referred to in Article 65 , the European Data Protection Supervisor shall have voting rights only on decisions which concern principles and rules applicable to the Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies which correspond in substance to those of this Regulation.

rules.action: act independently when performing its tasks or exercising its powers
rules.condition: pursuant to Articles 70 and 71
rules.scope: performing its tasks or exercising its powers pursuant to Articles 70 and 71
rules.raw_text: The Board shall act independently when performing its tasks or exercising its powers pursuant to Articles 70 and 71.

rules.action: neither seek nor take instructions from anybody
rules.condition: Without prejudice to requests by the Commission referred to in Article 70(1) and (2)
rules.scope: in the performance of its tasks or the exercise of its powers
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to requests by the Commission referred to in Article 70(1) and (2), the Board shall, in the performance of its tasks or the exercise of its powers, neither seek nor take instructions from anybody.

rules.action: ensure consistent application of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: 1 The Board shall ensure the consistent application of this Regulation.

rules.action: monitor and ensure correct application, advise Commission, issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices
rules.condition: at the request of the Commission or as per Articles 64 and 65
rules.raw_text: 2 monitor and ensure the correct application of this Regulation in the cases provided for in Articles 64 and 65

rules.action: advise Commission on issues related to data protection, amendments of Regulation
rules.raw_text: 2 advise the Commission on any issue related to the protection of personal data in the Union, including on any proposed amendment of this Regulation;

rules.action: advise on format and procedures for exchange of information between controllers, processors, supervisory authorities for binding corporate rules
rules.raw_text: 2 advise the Commission on the format and procedures for the exchange of information between controllers, processors and supervisory authorities for binding corporate rules;

rules.action: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices on erasing personal data from publicly available communication services
rules.raw_text: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices on procedures for erasing links, copies or replications of personal data from publicly available communication services as referred to in Article 17;

rules.action: examine questions covering the application of this Regulation, issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices
rules.condition: on its own initiative, on request of one of its members or on request of the Commission
rules.raw_text: examine, on its own initiative, on request of one of its members or on request of the Commission, any question covering the application of this Regulation and issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in order to encourage consistent application of this Regulation;

rules.action: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices for criteria and conditions for profiling decisions
rules.raw_text: issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for further specifying the criteria and conditions for decisions based on profiling pursuant to Article 22;

rules.action: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices for determining personal data breaches and undue delay
rules.raw_text: issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the particular circumstances in which a controller or a processor is required to notify the personal data breach;

rules.action: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices for determining high risk to rights and freedoms of natural persons
rules.raw_text: issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph as to the circumstances in which a personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of the natural persons referred to in Article 34;

rules.action: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices for criteria and requirements for personal data transfers based on binding corporate rules
rules.raw_text: issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements for personal data transfers based on binding corporate rules adhered to by controllers and binding corporate rules adhered to by processors and on further necessary requirements to ensure the protection of personal data of the data subjects concerned referred to in Article 47;

rules.action: issue guidelines, recommendations, best practices for criteria and requirements for personal data transfers on the basis of Article 49(1)
rules.raw_text: issue guidelines, recommendations and best practices in accordance with point (e) of this paragraph for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements for the personal data transfers on the basis of Article 49(1);

rules.action: draw up guidelines for supervisory authorities concerning application of measures and setting of administrative fines
rules.raw_text: draw up guidelines for supervisory authorities concerning the application of measures referred to in Article 58(1), (2) and (3) and the setting of administrative fines pursuant to Article 83;

rules.action: review practical application of guidelines, recommendations and best practices
rules.raw_text: review the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices;

rules.action: draw up an annual report
rules.condition: regarding the protection of natural persons with regard to processing in the Union and, where relevant, in third countries and international organisations
rules.raw_text: The Board shall draw up an annual report regarding the protection of natural persons with regard to processing in the Union and, where relevant, in third countries and international organisations.

rules.action: be made public and be transmitted
rules.condition: to the European Parliament, to the Council and to the Commission
rules.raw_text: The report shall be made public and be transmitted to the European Parliament, to the Council and to the Commission.

rules.action: include a review of
rules.condition: the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices referred to in point (l) of Article 70 (1) as well as of the binding decisions referred to in Article 65
rules.raw_text: The annual report shall include a review of the practical application of the guidelines, recommendations and best practices referred to in point (l) of Article 70 (1) as well as of the binding decisions referred to in Article 65.

rules.action: take decisions
rules.condition: by a simple majority of its members, unless otherwise provided for in this Regulation
rules.raw_text: The Board shall take decisions by a simple majority of its members, unless otherwise provided for in this Regulation.

rules.action: adopt rules of procedure
rules.condition: by a two-thirds majority of its members
rules.raw_text: The Board shall adopt its own rules of procedure by a two-thirds majority of its members.

rules.action: organise operational arrangements
rules.condition: its own operational arrangements
rules.raw_text: The Board shall organise its own operational arrangements.

rules.action: elect a chair and two deputy chairs
rules.condition: by simple majority
rules.raw_text: The Board shall elect a chair and two deputy chairs from amongst its members by simple majority.

rules.entity: The Chair and the deputy chairs
rules.action: have a term of office of five years
rules.scope: The Chair and the deputy chairs
rules.raw_text: The term of office of the Chair and of the deputy chairs shall be five years.

rules.entity: The Chair and the deputy chairs
rules.action: have renewable terms of office once
rules.scope: The Chair and the deputy chairs
rules.raw_text: and be renewable once.

action: to convene the meetings of the Board and prepare its agenda
to notify decisions adopted by the Board pursuant to Article 65 to the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned
to ensure the timely performance of the tasks of the Board, in particular in relation to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.
rules.scope: Within the scope of the GDPR and its specified articles
rules.raw_text: The Chair shall have the following tasks: to convene the meetings of the Board and prepare its agenda; to notify decisions adopted by the Board pursuant to Article 65 to the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned; to ensure the timely performance of the tasks of the Board, in particular in relation to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.

rules.action: have a secretariat
rules.condition: provided by the European Data Protection Supervisor
rules.scope: The tasks of the secretariat as specified in the article
rules.raw_text: The Board shall have a secretariat, which shall be provided by the European Data Protection Supervisor.

rules.action: perform its tasks exclusively under the instructions of the Chair of the Board
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: The tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation
rules.raw_text: The secretariat shall perform its tasks exclusively under the instructions of the Chair of the Board.

rules.entity: The staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation
rules.action: be subject to separate reporting lines from the staff involved in carrying out tasks conferred on the European Data Protection Supervisor
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: The staff as specified in the rule
rules.raw_text: The staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation shall be subject to separate reporting lines from the staff involved in carrying out tasks conferred on the European Data Protection Supervisor.

rules.entity: The Board and the European Data Protection Supervisor
rules.action: establish and publish a Memorandum of Understanding implementing this Article, determining the terms of their cooperation, and applicable to the staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation
rules.condition: Where appropriate
rules.scope: The implementation of Article 75 as specified in the rule
rules.raw_text: Where appropriate, the Board and the European Data Protection Supervisor shall establish and publish a Memorandum of Understanding implementing this Article, determining the terms of their cooperation, and applicable to the staff of the European Data Protection Supervisor involved in carrying out the tasks conferred on the Board by this Regulation.

rules.action: provide analytical, administrative and logistical support to the Board
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: The tasks as specified in the article
rules.raw_text: The secretariat shall provide analytical, administrative and logistical support to the Board.

rules.action: be responsible in particular for:
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: The tasks as specified in the article
rules.raw_text: The secretariat shall be responsible in particular for:

rules.action: handle the day-to-day business of the Board
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: the day-to-day business of the Board;

rules.action: handle communication between the members of the Board, its Chair and the Commission
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: communication between the members of the Board, its Chair and the Commission;

rules.action: handle communication with other institutions and the public
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: communication with other institutions and the public;

rules.action: handle the use of electronic means for internal and external communication
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: the use of electronic means for the internal and external communication;

rules.action: handle the translation of relevant information
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: the translation of relevant information;

rules.action: handle the preparation and follow-up of the meetings of the Board
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: the preparation and follow-up of the meetings of the Board;

rules.action: handle the preparation, drafting and publication of opinions, decisions on the settlement of disputes between supervisory authorities and other texts adopted by the Board
rules.condition: N/A
rules.raw_text: the preparation, drafting and publication of opinions, decisions on the settlement of disputes between supervisory authorities and other texts adopted by the Board.

rules.action: shall make discussions confidential where necessary
rules.condition: as provided for in its rules of procedure
rules.raw_text: The discussions of the Board shall be confidential where the Board deems it necessary, as provided for in its rules of procedure.

rules.entity: Documents submitted to members of the Board, experts and representatives of third parties
rules.action: Access shall be governed by Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001
rules.raw_text: Access to documents submitted to members of the Board, experts and representatives of third parties shall be governed by Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001

rules.action: Referenced Regulation
rules.raw_text: Regulation (EC) No 1049/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 30 May 2001 regarding public access to European Parliament, Council and Commission documents (OJ L 145, 31.5.2001, p. 43).

rules.action: lodge a complaint
rules.condition: considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation
rules.scope: with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement
rules.raw_text: Every data subject shall have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.

rules.entity: supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged
rules.action: inform the complainant on the progress and the outcome of the complaint including the possibility of a judicial remedy pursuant to Article 78
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authority with which the complaint has been lodged shall inform the complainant on the progress and the outcome of the complaint including the possibility of a judicial remedy pursuant to Article 78.

rules.action: have the right to an effective judicial remedy against a legally binding decision of a supervisory authority concerning them
rules.condition: none
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or non-judicial remedy, each natural or legal person shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy against a legally binding decision of a supervisory authority concerning them.

rules.action: have the right to an effective judicial remedy
rules.condition: when the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Articles 55 and 56 does not handle a complaint or does not inform the data subject within three months on the progress or outcome of the complaint lodged pursuant to Article 77.
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or non-judicial remedy, each data subject shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy where the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Articles 55 and 56 does not handle a complaint or does not inform the data subject within three months on the progress or outcome of the complaint lodged pursuant to Article 77.

rules.action: shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the supervisory authority is established
rules.condition: none
rules.raw_text: Proceedings against a supervisory authority shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the supervisory authority is established.

rules.action: shall forward that opinion or decision to the court
rules.condition: when proceedings are brought against a decision of a supervisory authority which was preceded by an opinion or a decision of the Board in the consistency mechanism
rules.raw_text: Where proceedings are brought against a decision of a supervisory authority which was preceded by an opinion or a decision of the Board in the consistency mechanism, the supervisory authority shall forward that opinion or decision to the court.

rules.action: have the right to an effective judicial remedy
rules.condition: where he or she considers that his or her rights under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing of his or her personal data in non-compliance with this Regulation
rules.scope: data subject's right to legal recourse
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to any available administrative or non-judicial remedy, including the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority pursuant to Article 77 , each data subject shall have the right to an effective judicial remedy where he or she considers that his or her rights under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing of his or her personal data in non-compliance with this Regulation.

rules.entity: proceedings against a controller or processor
rules.action: be brought before the courts of the Member State where the controller or processor has an establishment
rules.scope: venues for legal action against controllers and processors
rules.raw_text: Proceedings against a controller or a processor shall be brought before the courts of the Member State where the controller or processor has an establishment.

rules.entity: alternatively, such proceedings may be brought before the courts of the Member State where the data subject has his or her habitual residence
rules.condition: unless the controller or processor is a public authority of a Member State acting in the exercise of its public powers
rules.scope: additional venues for legal action against controllers and processors
rules.raw_text: Alternatively, such proceedings may be brought before the courts of the Member State where the data subject has his or her habitual residence, unless the controller or processor is a public authority of a Member State acting in the exercise of its public powers.

rules.action: mandate a not-for-profit body, organisation or association to lodge a complaint and exercise rights on their behalf
rules.condition: the body must be properly constituted in accordance with the law of a Member State, have statutory objectives which are in the public interest, and be active in the field of the protection of data subjects’ rights and freedoms with regard to the protection of their personal data
rules.scope: rights referred to in Articles 77, 78, and 79; right to receive compensation if provided for by Member State law
rules.raw_text: The data subject shall have the right to mandate a not-for-profit body, organisation or association which has been properly constituted in accordance with the law of a Member State, has statutory objectives which are in the public interest, and is active in the field of the protection of data subjects’ rights and freedoms with regard to the protection of their personal data to lodge the complaint on his or her behalf, to exercise the rights referred to in Articles 77 , 78 and 79 on his or her behalf, and to exercise the right to receive compensation referred to in Article 82 on his or her behalf where provided for by Member State law.

rules.entity: body, organisation or association mentioned in paragraph 1 of Article 80
rules.action: lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority independently of a data subject’s mandate and exercise rights if it considers that the rights of a data subject under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing
rules.raw_text: Member States may provide that any body, organisation or association referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, independently of a data subject’s mandate, has the right to lodge, in that Member State, a complaint with the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 77 and to exercise the rights referred to in Articles 78 and 79 if it considers that the rights of a data subject under this Regulation have been infringed as a result of the processing.

rules.entity: competent court of a Member State
rules.action: contact the court in another Member State to confirm the existence of proceedings
rules.condition: where proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing by the same controller or processor are pending in a court in another Member State
rules.scope: when a competent court has information on proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing by the same controller or processor that are pending in a court in another Member State
rules.raw_text: Where a competent court of a Member State has information on proceedings, concerning the same subject matter as regards processing by the same controller or processor, that are pending in a court in another Member State, it shall contact that court in the other Member State to confirm the existence of such proceedings.

rules.entity: any competent court other than the court first seized
rules.action: suspend its proceedings
rules.condition: where proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing of the same controller or processor are pending in a court in another Member State
rules.scope: when any competent court other than the court first seized has proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing of the same controller or processor that are pending in a court in another Member State
rules.raw_text: Where proceedings concerning the same subject matter as regards processing of the same controller or processor are pending in a court in another Member State, any competent court other than the court first seized may suspend its proceedings.

rules.entity: any court other than the court first seized
rules.action: decline jurisdiction
rules.condition: where proceedings are pending at first instance and the court first seized has jurisdiction over the actions in question and its law permits the consolidation thereof
rules.scope: when any court other than the court first seized has proceedings that are pending at first instance and the court first seized has jurisdiction over the actions in question and its law permits the consolidation thereof
rules.raw_text: Where those proceedings are pending at first instance, any court other than the court first seized may also, on the application of one of the parties, decline jurisdiction if the court first seized has jurisdiction over the actions in question and its law permits the consolidation thereof.

rules.entity: Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Regulation
rules.action: have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor for the damage suffered
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Any person who has suffered damage as a result of an infringement of GDPR
rules.raw_text: Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Regulation shall have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor for the damage suffered.

rules.action: be liable for the damage caused by processing which infringes this Regulation
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Any controller involved in processing that causes damage through an infringement of GDPR
rules.raw_text: Any controller involved in processing shall be liable for the damage caused by processing which infringes this Regulation.

rules.action: be liable for the damage caused by processing only where it has not complied with obligations of this Regulation specifically directed to processors or where it has acted outside or contrary to lawful instructions of the controller
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Processor that does not comply with GDPR's processor-specific obligations or acts outside or contrary to lawful controller instructions
rules.raw_text: A processor shall be liable for the damage caused by processing only where it has not complied with obligations of this Regulation specifically directed to processors or where it has acted outside or contrary to lawful instructions of the controller.

rules.action: be exempt from liability under paragraph 2 if it proves that it is not in any way responsible for the event giving rise to the damage
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Controller or processor who can prove they are not responsible for the damage-causing event
rules.raw_text: Where more than one controller or processor, or both a controller and a processor, are involved in the same processing and where they are, under paragraphs 2 and 3, responsible for any damage caused by processing, each controller or processor shall be held liable for the entire damage in order to ensure effective compensation of the data subject.

rules.entity: Controller or processor who has paid full compensation
rules.action: be entitled to claim back from the other controllers or processors involved in the same processing that part of the compensation corresponding to their part of responsibility for the damage
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Controller or processor who has paid full compensation can seek compensation from other parties involved
rules.raw_text: Where a controller or processor has, in accordance with paragraph 4, paid full compensation for the damage suffered, that controller or processor shall be entitled to claim back from the other controllers or processors involved in the same processing that part of the compensation corresponding to their part of responsibility for the damage, in accordance with the conditions set out in paragraph 2.

rules.action: be brought before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79 (2)
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation
rules.raw_text: Court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation shall be brought before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79 (2).

rules.action: impose administrative fines
rules.condition: infringements of this Regulation referred to in paragraphs 4, 5 and 6
rules.raw_text: Each supervisory authority shall ensure that the imposition of administrative fines pursuant to this Article in respect of infringements of this Regulation referred to in paragraphs 4, 5 and 6 shall in each individual case be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.

rules.action: be imposed in addition to or instead of measures
rules.condition: depending on the circumstances of each individual case
rules.raw_text: Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2).

rules.action: consider various factors when deciding on the amount of administrative fine
rules.condition: when deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case
rules.raw_text: When deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case due regard shall be given to the following:

rules.entity: total amount of the administrative fine
rules.action: not exceed the amount specified for the gravest infringement
rules.condition: if a controller or processor intentionally or negligently, for the same or linked processing operations, infringes several provisions of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: If a controller or processor intentionally or negligently, for the same or linked processing operations, infringes several provisions of this Regulation, the total amount of the administrative fine shall not exceed the amount specified for the gravest infringement.

rules.action: be up to 10 000 000 EUR or up to 2 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year (whichever is higher)
rules.condition: infringements of certain provisions
rules.raw_text: Infringements of the following provisions shall, in accordance with paragraph 2, be subject to administrative fines up to 10 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 2 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher:

rules.action: lay down the rules on other penalties applicable to infringements of this Regulation, take all measures necessary to ensure that they are implemented
rules.condition: for infringements which are not subject to administrative fines pursuant to Article 83
rules.raw_text: 1 Member States shall lay down the rules on other penalties applicable to infringements of this Regulation in particular for infringements which are not subject to administrative fines pursuant to Article 83 and shall take all measures necessary to ensure that they are implemented.

rules.action: notify the provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them to the Commission
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.

rules.action: be effective, proportionate and dissuasive
rules.raw_text: 2 Such penalties shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.

rules.action: provide for exemptions or derogations from various chapters of the GDPR
rules.condition: necessary to reconcile the right to the protection of personal data with the freedom of expression and information
rules.scope: for processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic, artistic or literary expression
rules.raw_text: Member States shall by law reconcile the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation with the right to freedom of expression and information, including processing for journalistic purposes and the purposes of academic, artistic or literary expression. For processing carried out for journalistic purposes or the purpose of academic artistic or literary expression, Member States shall provide for exemptions or derogations from Chapters II, III, IV, V, VI, VII and IX if they are necessary to reconcile the right to the protection of personal data with the freedom of expression and information.

rules.action: notify the Commission of provisions of its law which it has adopted pursuant to paragraph 1 and any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the provisions of its law which it has adopted pursuant to paragraph 2 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment law or amendment affecting them.

rules.entity: Public authority or body, private body
rules.action: Disclose personal data in official documents
rules.condition: In accordance with Union or Member State law to reconcile public access to official documents with the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation
rules.scope: For the performance of a task carried out in the public interest
rules.raw_text: Personal data in official documents held by a public authority or a public body or a private body for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest may be disclosed by the authority or body in accordance with Union or Member State law to which the public authority or body is subject in order to reconcile public access to official documents with the right to the protection of personal data pursuant to this Regulation.

rules.action: further determine the specific conditions for the processing of a national identification number or any other identifier of general application
rules.raw_text: Member States may further determine the specific conditions for the processing of a national identification number or any other identifier of general application.

rules.entity: National identification number or any other identifier of general application
rules.action: be used only under appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of the data subject pursuant to this Regulation
rules.raw_text: In that case the national identification number or any other identifier of general application shall be used only under appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of the data subject pursuant to this Regulation.

rules.action: provide more specific rules to ensure the protection of the rights and freedoms in respect of the processing of employees’ personal data in the employment context
rules.condition: for the purposes mentioned
rules.raw_text: Member States may, by law or by collective agreements, provide for more specific rules to ensure the protection of the rights and freedoms in respect of the processing of employees’ personal data in the employment context,

rules.action: include suitable and specific measures to safeguard the data subject’s human dignity, legitimate interests and fundamental rights
rules.condition: with particular regard to the transparency of processing, the transfer of personal data within a group of undertakings, or a group of enterprises engaged in a joint economic activity and monitoring systems at the work place
rules.raw_text: Those rules shall include suitable and specific measures to safeguard the data subject’s human dignity, legitimate interests and fundamental rights, with particular regard to the transparency of processing,

rules.action: notify to the Commission those provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them
rules.condition: None
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission those provisions of its law which it adopts pursuant to paragraph 1,

rules.entity: Processors for archiving purposes, scientific or historical research, or statistical purposes
rules.action: Implement appropriate safeguards
rules.condition: In accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject.
rules.raw_text: Processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, shall be subject to appropriate safeguards, in accordance with this Regulation, for the rights and freedoms of the data subject.

rules.action: Ensure technical and organisational measures to respect principle of data minimisation.
rules.raw_text: Those safeguards shall ensure that technical and organisational measures are in place in particular in order to ensure respect for the principle of data minimisation.

rules.action: Implement pseudonymisation if possible.
rules.condition: If those purposes can be fulfilled in that manner.
rules.raw_text: Those measures may include pseudonymisation provided that those purposes can be fulfilled in that manner.

rules.action: Process data without identifying subjects if possible.
rules.condition: If those purposes can be fulfilled by further processing which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects.
rules.raw_text: Where those purposes can be fulfilled by further processing which does not permit or no longer permits the identification of data subjects, those purposes shall be fulfilled in that manner.

rules.entity: Personal data for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes
rules.action: Derogate from rights in Articles 15, 16, 18 and 21
rules.condition: Union or Member State law provides and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes and likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes.
rules.raw_text: Where personal data are processed for scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.

rules.entity: Personal data for archiving purposes in the public interest
rules.action: Derogate from rights in Articles 15, 16, 18, 19, 20 and 21
rules.condition: Union or Member State law provides and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes and likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes.
rules.raw_text: Where personal data are processed for archiving purposes in the public interest, Union or Member State law may provide for derogations from the rights referred to in Articles 15, 16, 18, 19, 20 and 21 subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article in so far as such rights are likely to render impossible or seriously impair the achievement of the specific purposes, and such derogations are necessary for the fulfilment of those purposes.

rules.entity: Processing referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3
rules.action: Apply derogations only to processing for the purposes mentioned in those paragraphs.
rules.raw_text: Where processing referred to in paragraphs 2 and 3 serves at the same time another purpose, the derogations shall apply only to processing for the purposes referred to in those paragraphs.

rules.action: adopt specific rules
rules.condition: to set out the powers of the supervisory authorities laid down in points (e) and (f) of Article 58 in relation to controllers or processors that are subject to an obligation of professional secrecy or other equivalent obligations of secrecy where this is necessary and proportionate to reconcile the right of the protection of personal data with the obligation of secrecy
rules.scope: personal data which the controller or processor has received as a result of or has obtained in an activity covered by that obligation of secrecy
rules.raw_text: 1 Member States may adopt specific rules to set out the powers of the supervisory authorities laid down in points (e) and (f) of Article 58

rules.action: notify to the Commission
rules.condition: the rules adopted pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall notify to the Commission the rules adopted pursuant to paragraph 1, by 25 May 2018 and, without delay, any subsequent amendment affecting them.

rules.entity: Churches and religious associations or communities that apply comprehensive rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to processing
rules.action: may continue to apply their comprehensive rules provided they are brought into line with this Regulation
rules.condition: at the time of entry into force of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: Where in a Member State, churches and religious associations or communities apply, at the time of entry into force of this Regulation, comprehensive rules relating to the protection of natural persons with regard to processing, such rules may continue to apply, provided that they are brought into line with this Regulation.

rules.entity: Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 of Article 91
rules.action: are subject to the supervision of an independent supervisory authority
rules.condition: which may be specific, provided that it fulfils the conditions laid down in Chapter VI of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: Churches and religious associations which apply comprehensive rules in accordance with paragraph 1 of this Article shall be subject to the supervision of an independent supervisory authority, which may be specific, provided that it fulfils the conditions laid down in Chapter VI of this Regulation.

rules.action: adopt delegated acts
rules.condition: subject to the conditions laid down in this Article
rules.raw_text: The power to adopt delegated acts is conferred on the Commission subject to the conditions laid down in this Article.

rules.action: revoke delegation of power
rules.condition: at any time
rules.scope: applies to the delegation referred in Article 12(8) and Article 43(8)
rules.raw_text: The delegation of power referred to in Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) may be revoked at any time by the European Parliament or by the Council.

rules.action: express objection
rules.condition: within a period of three months of notification of that act to the European Parliament and the Council or if, before the expiry of that period, the European Parliament and the Council have both informed the Commission that they will not object.
rules.scope: applies to a delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 12(8) and Article 43(8)
rules.raw_text: A delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 12(8) and Article 43(8) shall enter into force only if no objection has been expressed by either the European Parliament or the Council within a period of three months of notification of that act to the European Parliament and the Council...

rules.action: be assisted by a committee
rules.scope: The Commission according to GDPR article 93
rules.raw_text: 1 The Commission shall be assisted by a committee.

rules.action: Replace references to Directive 95/46/EC with this Regulation
rules.condition: The replacement occurs from 25 May 2018
rules.raw_text: Directive 95/46/EC is repealed with effect from 25 May 2018.

rules.action: Replace references to the Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data established by Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC with the European Data Protection Board established by this Regulation
rules.condition: The replacement occurs from 25 May 2018
rules.raw_text: References to the Working Party on the Protection of Individuals with regard to the Processing of Personal Data established by Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC shall be construed as references to the European Data Protection Board established by this Regulation.

rules.entity: natural or legal persons providing publicly available electronic communications services in public communication networks in the Union
rules.action: are not imposed additional obligations by this Regulation
rules.condition: in relation to matters for which they are subject to specific obligations with the same objective set out in Directive 2002/58/EC
rules.scope: processing activities related to the provision of such services
rules.raw_text: This Regulation shall not impose additional obligations on natural or legal persons in relation to processing in connection with the provision of publicly available electronic communications services in public communication networks in the Union in relation to matters for which they are subject to specific obligations with the same objective set out in Directive 2002/58/EC.

rules.action: International agreements involving the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations remain in force until amended, replaced or revoked.
rules.condition: The agreements were concluded by Member States prior to 24 May 2016 and comply with Union law as applicable prior to that date.
rules.scope: International agreements involving the transfer of personal data
rules.raw_text: International agreements involving the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations which were concluded by Member States prior to 24 May 2016, and which comply with Union law as applicable prior to that date, shall remain in force until amended, replaced or revoked.

rules.action: submit a report on the evaluation and review of this Regulation
rules.condition: By 25 May 2020 and every four years thereafter
rules.scope: to the European Parliament and to the Council
rules.raw_text: By 25 May 2020 and every four years thereafter, the Commission shall submit a report on the evaluation and review of this Regulation to the European Parliament and to the Council.

rules.action: make public the reports
rules.raw_text: The reports shall be made public.

rules.action: examine the application and functioning of specified chapters
rules.condition: In the context of the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraph 1
rules.scope: Chapter V on the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations with particular regard to decisions adopted pursuant to Article 45(3) of this Regulation and decisions adopted on the basis of Article 25(6) of Directive 95/46/EC; Chapter VII on cooperation and consistency.
rules.raw_text: In the context of the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraph 1, the Commission shall examine, in particular, the application and functioning of:

rules.action: request information from Member States and supervisory authorities
rules.condition: For the purpose of paragraph 1
rules.scope: from Member States and supervisory authorities
rules.raw_text: The Commission may request information from Member States and supervisory authorities.

rules.action: take into account the positions and findings of specified bodies or sources
rules.condition: In carrying out the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.scope: the positions and findings of the European Parliament, of the Council, and of other relevant bodies or sources.
rules.raw_text: In carrying out the evaluations and reviews referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, the Commission shall take into account the positions and findings of the European Parliament, of the Council, and of other relevant bodies or sources.

rules.action: submit appropriate proposals to amend this Regulation
rules.condition: if necessary
rules.scope: in particular taking into account of developments in information technology and in the light of the state of progress in the information society.
rules.raw_text: The Commission shall, if necessary, submit appropriate proposals to amend this Regulation,

rules.action: submit legislative proposals with a view to amending other Union legal acts on the protection of personal data
rules.condition: if appropriate
rules.scope: Union legal acts on the protection of personal data, particularly those concerning processing by Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies and free movement of such data
rules.raw_text: The Commission shall, if appropriate, submit legislative proposals with a view to amending other Union legal acts on the protection of personal data, in order to ensure uniform and consistent protection of natural persons with regard to processing.

rules.action: enter into force
rules.condition: on the twentieth day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union
rules.raw_text: This Regulation shall enter into force on the twentieth day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union

rules.action: apply
rules.condition: from 25 May 2018
rules.raw_text: It shall apply from 25 May 2018

rules.action: provides for the harmonisation of national provisions required to ensure an equivalent level of protection of fundamental rights and freedoms, and in particular the right to privacy and confidentiality, with respect to the processing of personal data in the electronic communication sector and to ensure the free movement of such data and of electronic communication equipment and services in the Community.
rules.raw_text: This Directive provides for the harmonisation of the national provisions required to ensure an equivalent level of protection of fundamental rights and freedoms, and in particular the right to privacy and confidentiality, with respect to the processing of personal data in the electronic communication sector and to ensure the free movement of such data and of electronic communication equipment and services in the Community.

rules.action: particularise and complement Directive 95/46/EC for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 1. Moreover, they provide for protection of the legitimate interests of subscribers who are legal persons.
rules.raw_text: The provisions of this Directive particularise and complement Directive 95/46/EC for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 1. Moreover, they provide for protection of the legitimate interests of subscribers who are legal persons.

rules.action: shall not apply to activities which fall outside the scope of the Treaty establishing the European Community, such as those covered by Titles V and VI of the Treaty on European Union, and in any case to activities concerning public security, defence, State security (including the economic well-being of the State when the activities relate to State security matters) and the activities of the State in areas of criminal law.
rules.raw_text: This Directive shall not apply to activities which fall outside the scope of the Treaty establishing the European Community, such as those covered by Titles V and VI of the Treaty on European Union, and in any case to activities concerning public security, defence, State security (including the economic well-being of the State when the activities relate to State security matters) and the activities of the State in areas of criminal law.

rules.entity: any natural person using a publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: applies the definitions
rules.condition: Save as otherwise provided, the definitions in Directive 95/46/EC and in Directive 2002/21/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 7 March 2002 on a common regulatory framework for electronic communications networks and services (Framework Directive) apply
rules.scope: the definitions in the given directives apply to the given entity
rules.raw_text: 'user' means any natural person using a publicly available electronic communications service

rules.entity: any data processed for the purpose of the conveyance of a communication or billing
rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.scope: the given data applies to the definition of 'traffic data'
rules.raw_text: 'traffic data' means any data processed for the purpose of the conveyance of a communication on an electronic communications network or for the billing thereof

rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.scope: 'location data' applies to any data processed in an electronic communications network or by an electronic communications service indicating the geographic position of the terminal equipment of a user of a publicly available electronic communications service
rules.raw_text: 'location data' means any data processed in an electronic communications network or by an electronic communications service, indicating the geographic position of the terminal equipment of a user of a publicly available electronic communications service

rules.entity: any information exchanged or conveyed between a finite number of parties by means of a publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.raw_text: 'communication' means any information exchanged or conveyed between a finite number of parties by means of a publicly available electronic communications service

rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.scope: 'consent' by a user or subscriber corresponds to the data subject's consent in Directive 95/46/EC
rules.raw_text: 'consent' by a user or subscriber corresponds to the data subject's consent in Directive 95/46/EC

rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.scope: 'value added service' means any service which requires the processing of traffic data or location data other than traffic data beyond what is necessary for the transmission of a communication or the billing thereof
rules.raw_text: 'value added service' means any service which requires the processing of traffic data or location data other than traffic data beyond what is necessary for the transmission of a communication or the billing thereof

rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.scope: 'electronic mail' means any text, voice, sound or image message sent over a public communications network which can be stored in the network or in the recipient's terminal equipment until it is collected by the recipient
rules.raw_text: 'electronic mail' means any text, voice, sound or image message sent over a public communications network which can be stored in the network or in the recipient's terminal equipment until it is collected by the recipient

rules.action: applies the definition of
rules.scope: 'personal data breach' means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed in connection with the provision of a publicly available electronic communications service in the Community
rules.raw_text: 'personal data breach' means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed in connection with the provision of a publicly available electronic communications service in the Community

rules.action: applies to the processing of personal data in connection with provision
rules.condition: in publically available electronic communications services in public communications networks in the Community
rules.scope: including public communications networks supporting data collection and identification devices
rules.raw_text: This Directive shall apply to the processing of personal data in connection with the provision of publicly available electronic communications services in public communications networks in the Community, including public communications networks supporting data collection and identification devices.

rules.entity: provider of a publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: take appropriate technical and organisational measures to safeguard security of its services
rules.condition: if necessary in conjunction with the provider of the public communications network with respect to network security. Having regard to the state of the art and the cost of their implementation, these measures shall ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk presented.
rules.raw_text: The provider of a publicly available electronic communications service must take appropriate technical and organisational measures to safeguard security of its services, if necessary in conjunction with the provider of the public communications network with respect to network security. Having regard to the state of the art and the cost of their implementation, these measures shall ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk presented.

rules.entity: provider of a publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: inform subscribers concerning a risk of breach of the security of the network
