rules.condition: in case of a particular risk of a breach of the security of the network
rules.raw_text: In case of a particular risk of a breach of the security of the network, the provider of a publicly available electronic communications service must inform the subscribers concerning such risk and, where the risk lies outside the scope of the measures to be taken by the service provider, of any possible remedies, including an indication of the likely costs involved.

rules.entity: provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: notify the competent national authority and subscribers of a personal data breach
rules.condition: when a personal data breach occurs
rules.scope: competent national authority and subscribers concerned
rules.raw_text: 3.   In the case of a personal data breach, the provider of publicly available electronic communications services shall, without undue delay, notify the personal data breach to the competent national authority.

rules.entity: provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: notify the subscriber or individual of a personal data breach
rules.condition: when the personal data breach is likely to adversely affect the personal data or privacy of a subscriber or individual
rules.raw_text: 3.   In the case of a personal data breach, the provider of publicly available electronic communications services shall, without undue delay, notify the personal data breach to the competent national authority. When the personal data breach is likely to adversely affect the personal data or privacy of a subscriber or individual, the provider shall also notify the subscriber or individual of the breach without undue delay.

rules.entity: provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: implement appropriate technological protection measures
rules.condition: to be exempt from notifying a subscriber or individual of the personal data breach
rules.scope: personal data breach notification to a subscriber or individual concerned
rules.raw_text: Notification of a personal data breach to a subscriber or individual concerned shall not be required if the provider has demonstrated to the satisfaction of the competent authority that it has implemented appropriate technological protection measures, and that those measures were applied to the data concerned by the security breach. Such technological protection measures shall render the data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it.

rules.action: adopt technical implementing measures
rules.condition: concerning the circumstances, format and procedures applicable to the information and notification requirements referred to in this Article
rules.raw_text: Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC and the European Data Protection Supervisor, adopt technical implementing measures concerning the circumstances, format and procedures applicable to the information and notification requirements referred to in this Article.

rules.action: shall involve all relevant stakeholders
rules.condition: in order to be informed of the best available technical and economic means of implementation of this Article.
rules.raw_text: Those measures, designed to amend non-essential elements of this Directive by supplementing it, shall be adopted in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14a(2).

rules.action: ensure confidentiality of communications and the related traffic data
rules.condition: through national legislation
rules.scope: public communications network and publicly available electronic communications services
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure the confidentiality of communications and the related traffic data by means of a public communications network and publicly available electronic communications services, through national legislation.

rules.action: not prevent
rules.condition: any technical storage or access for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network, or as strictly necessary in order for the provider of an information society service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user to provide the service
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States shall ensure that the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent, having been provided with clear and comprehensive information, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, unless it is necessary for technical storage or access for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network, or as strictly necessary in order for the provider of an information society service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user to provide the service.

rules.action: receive non-itemised bills

rules.action: offer the possibility of preventing presentation of calling line identification on a per-call basis to the calling user, free of charge
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the calling user the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing presentation of calling line identification of incoming calls, free of charge for reasonable use
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge for reasonable use of this function, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification of incoming calls.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of rejecting incoming calls where calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered and when the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered and where the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means, of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing the presentation of connected line identification to the calling user, free of charge
rules.condition: when presentation of connected line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of connected line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the connected line identification to the calling user.

rules.action: process location data other than traffic data
rules.condition: when made anonymous or with the consent of users/subscribers for the provision of a value added service

rules.condition: none
rules.scope: location data other than traffic data
rules.raw_text: The service provider must inform the users or subscribers, prior to obtaining their consent, of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value added service.

rules.action: refuse processing of location data other than traffic data temporarily
rules.condition: none
rules.scope: each connection to the network or for each transmission of a communication
rules.raw_text: Where consent of the users or subscribers has been obtained for the processing of location data other than traffic data, the user or subscriber must continue to have the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of temporarily refusing the processing of such data for each connection to the network or for each transmission of a communication.

rules.entity: persons processing location data other than traffic data

rules.condition: none
rules.scope: processing in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.raw_text: Processing of location data other than traffic data in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2 must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of the provider of the public communications network or publicly available communications service or of the third party providing the value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of providing the value added service.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: subscribers are informed about the purpose of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers and any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory, before they are included in the directory.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about the purpose(s) of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers available to the public or obtainable through directory enquiry services, in which their personal data can be included and of any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: subscribers are given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, verify, correct or withdraw such data, and not being included, verifying, correcting or withdrawing personal data from it shall be free of charge.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that subscribers are given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, and if so, which, to the extent that such data are relevant for the purpose of the directory as determined by the provider of the directory, and to verify, correct or withdraw such data. Not being included in a public subscriber directory, verifying, correcting or withdrawing personal data from it shall be free of charge.

rules.action: may require
rules.condition: additional consent be asked of the subscribers for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers.
rules.raw_text: Member States may require that for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers, additional consent be asked of the subscribers.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall also ensure, in the framework of Community law and applicable national legislation, that the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.

rules.entity: automatic calling machines, facsimile machines (fax) or electronic mail
rules.action: use for direct marketing may be allowed only in respect of subscribers or users who have given their prior consent.
rules.raw_text: The use of automated calling and communication systems without human intervention (automatic calling machines), facsimile machines (fax) or electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing may be allowed only in respect of subscribers or users who have given their prior consent.

rules.action: use these electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services
rules.condition: customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object, free of charge and in an easy manner, to such use of electronic contact details at the time of their collection and on the occasion of each message in case the customer has not initially refused such use.
rules.raw_text: Notwithstanding paragraph 1, where a natural or legal person obtains from its customers their electronic contact details for electronic mail, in the context of the sale of a product or a service, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, the same natural or legal person may use these electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services provided that customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object, free of charge and in an easy manner, to such use of electronic contact details at the time of their collection and on the occasion of each message in case the customer has not initially refused such use.

rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that any subscriber has the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of stopping automatic call forwarding by a third party to the subscriber's terminal.

rules.action: ensure that subscribers are informed about the purpose of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers, and given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in it, verify, correct or withdraw such data.
rules.condition: before they are included in the directory and relevant for the purpose of the directory
rules.raw_text: 1.   Member States shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about the purpose(s) of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers available to the public or obtainable through directory enquiry services, in which their personal data can be included and of any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory. 2.   Member States shall ensure that subscribers are given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, and if so, which, to the extent that such data are relevant for the purpose of the directory as determined by the provider of the directory, and to verify, correct or withdraw such data. Not being included in a public subscriber directory, verifying, correcting or withdrawing personal data from it shall be free of charge.

rules.action: may require that for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers, additional consent be asked of the subscribers.
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States may require that for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers, additional consent be asked of the subscribers.

rules.action: shall also ensure, in the framework of Community law and applicable national legislation, that the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.
rules.raw_text: 4.   Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall apply to subscribers who are natural persons. Member States shall also ensure, in the framework of Community law and applicable national legislation, that the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.

rules.raw_text: Where personal data relating to a data subject are collected from the data subject, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with all of the following information:

rules.action: request confirmation of data processing activities, or access to their personal data
rules.condition: the data subject has made a written, electronic or oral request
rules.raw_text: Data subjects have the right to obtain from the controller confirmation as to whether or not personal data concerning them are being processed. They also have the right to request from the controller access to their personal data and the following information: (a) the purposes of the processing; (b) the categories of personal data concerned; (c) the recipients or categories of recipient to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed, in particular recipients in third countries or international organisations; (d) where possible, the envisaged period for which the personal data will be stored, or, if not possible, the criteria used to determine that period; (e) the existence of the right to request from the controller rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing of personal data concerning the data subject or to object to such processing; (f) the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority; (g) where the personal data are not collected from the data subject, any available information as to their source; (h) the existence of automated decision-making, including profiling, referred to in Article 22(1) and (4) and, at least in those cases, meaningful information about the logic involved, as well as the significance and the envisaged consequences of such processing for the data subject.

rules.raw_text: Any judgment of a court or tribunal and any decision of an administrative authority of a third country requiring a controller or processor to transfer or disclose personal data may only be recognised or enforceable in any manner if based on an international agreement, such as a mutual legal assistance treaty, in force between the requesting third country and the Union or a Member State, without prejudice to other grounds for transfer pursuant to this Chapter.

rules.action: is informed about the transfer
rules.condition: the data transfer is not repetitive, concerns only a limited number of data subjects, and is necessary for important reasons of public interest
rules.raw_text: The controller shall inform the supervisory authority of the transfer and the data subject of the transfer and on the compelling legitimate interests pursued. A transfer pursuant to point (g) of the first subparagraph of paragraph 1 shall not involve the entirety of the personal data or entire categories of the personal data contained in the register. Where the register is intended for consultation by persons having a legitimate interest, the controller shall ensure that the consultation does not result in the disclosure of personal data to unauthorised third parties. An inadvertent disclosure of personal data, which is made as a result of the application of a law, including a statute of limitation, shall not be considered a violation of this Regulation.

rules.action: take appropriate steps
rules.condition: in relation to third countries and international organisations
rules.scope: develop international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate the effective enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data
rules.raw_text: In relation to third countries and international organisations, the Commission and supervisory authorities shall take appropriate steps to develop international cooperation mechanisms to facilitate the effective enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data.

rules.action: provide international mutual assistance
rules.condition: in the enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data
rules.scope: [subject to appropriate safeguards for the protection of personal data and other fundamental rights and freedoms]
rules.raw_text: The Commission and supervisory authorities shall provide international mutual assistance in the enforcement of legislation for the protection of personal data, including through notification, complaint referral, investigative assistance and information exchange, subject to appropriate safeguards for the protection of personal data and other fundamental rights and freedoms.

rules.action: monitor the application of GDPR
rules.condition: in order to protect the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall provide for one or more independent public authorities to be responsible for monitoring the application of this Regulation, in order to protect the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons in relation to processing and to facilitate the free flow of personal data within the Union.

rules.entity: Anyone processing personal data (automated or non-automated and part of a filing system)

rules.raw_text: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data wholly or partly by automated means and to the processing other than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system or are intended to form part of a filing system.

rules.action: The Regulation does not apply
rules.raw_text: This Regulation does not apply to: 1) the processing of personal data in the course of an activity which falls outside the scope of Union law; 2) by the Member States when carrying out activities which fall within the scope of Chapter 2 of Title V of the TEU; 3) by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity; 4) by competent authorities for the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including the safeguarding against and the prevention of threats to public security.

rules.entity: Processing by Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies

rules.raw_text: Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 and other Union legal acts applicable to such processing of personal data shall be adapted to the principles and rules of this Regulation in accordance with Article 98.

rules.raw_text: This Regulation shall be without prejudice to the application of Directive 2000/31/EC, in particular of the liability rules of intermediary service providers in Articles 12 to 15 of that Directive.

rules.action: defining the meaning of processing
rules.condition: any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data, whether automated or not
rules.raw_text: 'processing’ means any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data or on sets of personal data, whether or not by automated means,

rules.action: determining the purposes and means of processing
rules.condition: alone or jointly with others
rules.raw_text: 'controller’ means the natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others,

rules.action: processing personal data on behalf of the controller
rules.raw_text: 'processor’ means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which processes personal data on behalf of the controller;

rules.action: receiving personal data, whether a third party or not
rules.raw_text: 'recipient’ means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or another body,

rules.action: not considered recipients when receiving personal data in the framework of a particular inquiry
rules.condition: in accordance with Union or Member State law
rules.raw_text: However, public authorities which may receive personal data in the framework of a particular inquiry in accordance with Union or Member State law shall not be regarded as recipients;

rules.action: defining the meaning of restriction of processing
rules.condition: marking of stored personal data with the aim of limiting their processing in the future
rules.raw_text: 'restriction of processing’ means the marking of stored personal data with the aim of limiting their processing in the future;

rules.action: defining the meaning of profiling
rules.condition: any form of automated processing of personal data consisting of the use of personal data to evaluate certain personal aspects
rules.raw_text: 'profiling’ means any form of automated processing of personal data consisting of the use of personal data to evaluate certain personal aspects relating to a natural person;

rules.action: defining the meaning of pseudonymisation
rules.condition: processing of personal data in such a manner that the personal data can no longer be attributed to a specific data subject without the use of additional information
rules.raw_text: 'pseudonymisation’ means the processing of personal data in such a manner that the personal data can no longer be attributed to a specific data subject without the use of additional information,

rules.action: defining the meaning of filing system
rules.condition: any structured set of personal data which are accessible according to specific criteria
rules.raw_text: 'filing system’ means any structured set of personal data which are accessible according to specific criteria,

rules.action: refers to an identified or identifiable natural person
rules.raw_text: 'data subject’ means any identified or identifiable natural person;

rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data for unique identification of a natural person, data concerning health or sex life/sexual orientation
rules.scope: unless one of the exceptions listed below applies
rules.raw_text: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or trade union membership, and the processing of genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person’s sex life or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.

rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: specified purposes, Union or Member State law prevents data subject from lifting prohibition
rules.raw_text: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject;

rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: necessary for carrying out obligations, exercising specific rights in employment and social security law, authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement
rules.scope: processing is necessary for the purposes of carrying out the obligations and exercising specific rights of the controller or of the data subject in the field of employment and social security and social protection law
rules.raw_text: processing is necessary for the purposes of carrying out the obligations and exercising specific rights of the controller or of the data subject in the field of employment and social security and social protection law in so far as it is authorised by Union or Member State law or a collective agreement pursuant to Member State law providing for appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;

rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards, political, philosophical, religious or trade union aim, data subject's consent not disclosed outside the body
rules.scope: processing relates to members or former members of the body or to persons who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects
rules.raw_text: processing is carried out in the course of its legitimate activities with appropriate safeguards by a foundation, association or any other not-for-profit body with a political, philosophical, religious or trade union aim and on condition that the processing relates solely to the members or to former members of the body or to persons who have regular contact with it in connection with its purposes and that the personal data are not disclosed outside that body without the consent of the data subjects;

rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: personal data made public by the data subject
rules.scope: processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject
rules.raw_text: processing is necessary for reasons of substantial public interest, on the basis of Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued, respect the essence of the right to data protection and provide for suitable and specific measures to safeguard the fundamental rights and the interests of the data subject;

rules.entity: courts acting in their judicial capacity
rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims
rules.scope: processing is necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims or whenever courts are acting in their judicial capacity
rules.raw_text: processing is necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, such as protecting against serious cross-border threats to health or ensuring high standards o

rules.action: Processing
rules.condition: necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, Union or Member State law
rules.scope: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3
rules.raw_text: processing is necessary for reasons of public interest in the area of public health, such as protecting against serious cross-border threats to health or ensuring high standards o

rules.action: provide information
rules.condition: relating to processing
rules.raw_text: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information referred to in Articles 13 and 14 and any communication under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 relating to processing to the data subject in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language, in particular for any information addressed specifically to a child.

rules.action: provide information
rules.condition: by electronic means or writing
rules.raw_text: The information shall be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means.

rules.action: provide information orally
rules.condition: requested by data subject and identity proven by other means
rules.raw_text: When requested by the data subject, the information may be provided orally, provided that the identity of the data subject is proven by other means.

rules.action: facilitate exercise of data subject rights
rules.raw_text: The controller shall facilitate the exercise of data subject rights under Articles 15 to 22.

rules.action: not refuse to act on request
rules.condition: unless controller demonstrates that it is not in a position to identify data subject
rules.raw_text: In the cases referred to in Article 11(2), the controller shall not refuse to act on the request of the data subject for exercising his or her rights under Articles 15 to 22, unless the controller demonstrates that it is not in a position to identify the data subject.

rules.action: provide information on action taken
rules.raw_text: The controller shall provide information on action taken on a request under Articles 15 to 22 to the data subject without undue delay and in any event within one month of receipt of the request.

rules.action: extend time for providing information
rules.condition: complexity and number of requests
rules.raw_text: That period may be extended by two further months where necessary, taking into account the complexity and number of the requests.

rules.action: inform data subject of extension
rules.raw_text: The controller shall inform the data subject of any such extension within one month of receipt of the request, together with the reasons for the delay.

rules.action: provide information by electronic means
rules.condition: request made by electronic form means
rules.raw_text: Where the data subject makes the request by electronic form means, the information shall be provided by electronic means where possible, unless otherwise requested by the data subject.

rules.action: inform data subject of non-action
rules.condition: does not take action on request
rules.raw_text: If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject without delay and at the latest within one month of receipt of the request of the reasons for not taking action and on the possibility of lodging a complaint with a supervisory authority and seeking a judicial remedy.

rules.action: provide information free of charge
rules.raw_text: Information provided under Articles 13 and 14 and any communication and any actions taken under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 shall be provided free of charge.

rules.action: charge reasonable fee or refuse request
rules.condition: manifestly unfounded or excessive requests
rules.raw_text: Where requests from a data subject are manifestly unfounded or excessive, in particular because of their repetitive character, the controller may either: charge a reasonable fee taking into account the administrative costs of providing the information or communication or taking the action requested; or refuse to act on the request.

rules.action: burden of demonstrating manifestly unfounded or excessive request
rules.raw_text: The controller shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive character of the request.

rules.action: request additional information for identity confirmation
rules.condition: reasonable doubts concerning identity of natural person making request
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to Article 11, where the controller has reasonable doubts concerning the identity of the natural person making the request referred to in Articles 15 to 21, the controller may request the provision of additional information necessary to confirm the identity of the data subject.

rules.action: provide information
rules.condition: personal data not obtained from the data subject
rules.raw_text: the controller shall provide the data subject with the following information:

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: data processing for which personal data are intended
rules.raw_text: the purposes of the processing for which the personal data are intended as well as the legal basis for the processing

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: categories of personal data concerned
rules.raw_text: the categories of personal data concerned

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data
rules.raw_text: the recipients or categories of recipients of the personal data, if any

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation
rules.raw_text: where applicable, that the controller intends to transfer personal data to a recipient in a third country or international organisation and the existence or absence of an adequacy decision by the Commission

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: ensure fair and transparent processing
rules.raw_text: the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if that is not possible, the criteria used to determine that period

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: processing based on point (f) of Article 6(1)
rules.raw_text: where the processing is based on point (f) of Article 6(1), the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: processing is based on point (a) of Article 6(1) or point (a) of Article 9(2)
rules.raw_text: the existence of the right to withdraw consent at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: from which source the personal data originate
rules.raw_text: from which source the personal data originate, and if applicable, whether it came from publicly accessible sources

rules.action: provide specific information
rules.condition: automated decision-making, including profiling
rules.raw_text: the existence of automated decision-making, including profiling, referred to in Article 22(1) and (4)

rules.action: provide information
rules.condition: personal data obtained, communication with the data subject, or disclosure to another recipient
rules.raw_text: The controller shall provide the information referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2:

rules.action: provide information within a reasonable period
rules.condition: personal data obtained
rules.raw_text: within a reasonable period after obtaining the personal data, but at the latest within one month, having regard to the specific circumstances in which the personal data are processed

rules.action: provide information at the time of first communication
rules.condition: personal data to be used for communication with the data subject
rules.raw_text: if the personal data are to be used for communication with the data subject, at the latest at the time of the first communication to that data subject

rules.action: provide information when disclosing personal data
rules.condition: disclosure to another recipient is envisaged
rules.raw_text: if a disclosure to another recipient is envisaged, at the latest when the personal data are first disclosed

rules.action: communicate rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing
rules.condition: carried out in accordance with Article 16, Article 17(1) and Article 18
rules.scope: to each recipient to whom the personal data have been disclosed
rules.raw_text: The controller shall communicate any rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing carried out in accordance with Article 16, Article 17(1) and Article 18 to each recipient to whom the personal data have been disclosed, unless this proves impossible or involves disproportionate effort.

rules.action: inform the data subject about those recipients
rules.condition: if the data subject requests it
rules.raw_text: The controller shall inform the data subject about those recipients if the data subject requests it.

rules.entity: <processor or person acting under the authority of controller or processor>
rules.action: process personal data
rules.condition: has access to personal data and is not instructed by the controller, unless required by Union or Member State law
rules.raw_text: The processor and any person acting under the authority of the controller or of the processor, who has access to personal data, shall not process those data except on instructions from the controller, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law.

rules.entity: Each controller and where applicable the controller’s representative
rules.action: maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility
rules.scope: Applicable to controllers and their representatives
rules.raw_text: Each controller and, where applicable, the controller’s representative, shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility.

rules.entity: The record maintained by the controller
rules.action: contain all of the following information
rules.scope: Information to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: That record shall contain all of the following information:

rules.action: the name and contact details of the controller, data protection officer, etc.
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: the name and contact details of the controller and, where applicable, the joint controller, the controller’s representative and the data protection officer;

rules.action: the purposes of the processing
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: the purposes of the processing;

rules.action: a description of the categories of data subjects and of the categories of personal data
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: a description of the categories of data subjects and of the categories of personal data;

rules.action: the categories of recipients to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed including recipients in third countries or international organisations
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: the categories of recipients to whom the personal data have been or will be disclosed including recipients in third countries or international organisations;

rules.action: where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, etc.
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, including the identification of that third country or international organisation and, in the case of transfers referred to in the second subparagraph of Article 49 (1), the documentation of suitable safeguards;

rules.action: where possible, the envisaged time limits for erasure of the different categories of data
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32 (1).

rules.entity: Each processor and where applicable the processor’s representative
rules.action: maintain a record of all categories of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller, containing:
rules.scope: Information to be included in the record of processing activities by processors
rules.raw_text: Each processor and, where applicable, the processor’s representative shall maintain a record of all categories of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller, containing:

rules.action: the name and contact details of the processor or processors and of each controller on behalf of which the processor is acting, etc.
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities by processors
rules.raw_text: the name and contact details of the processor or processors and of each controller on behalf of which the processor is acting, and, where applicable, of the controller’s or the processor’s representative, and the data protection officer;

rules.action: the categories of processing carried out on behalf of each controller
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities by processors
rules.raw_text: the categories of processing carried out on behalf of each controller;

rules.action: where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, etc.
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities by processors
rules.raw_text: where applicable, transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, including the identification of that third country or international organisation and, in the case of transfers referred to in the second subparagraph of Article 49 (1), the documentation of suitable safeguards;

rules.action: where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32 (1)
rules.scope: Details to be included in the record of processing activities by processors
rules.raw_text: where possible, a general description of the technical and organisational security measures referred to in Article 32 (1).

rules.entity: The records referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.action: be in writing, including in electronic form
rules.scope: Format of the record of processing activities
rules.raw_text: The records referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.

rules.entity: The controller or the processor and where applicable the controller’s or the processor’s representative
rules.action: make the record available to the supervisory authority on request
rules.scope: Availability of the record of processing activities for the supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: The controller or the processor and, where applicable, the controller’s or the processor’s representative, shall make the record available to the supervisory authority on request.

rules.entity: The obligations referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.action: not apply to an enterprise or an organisation employing fewer than 250 persons unless the processing it carries out is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects, etc.
rules.scope: Exceptions to the obligations in paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.raw_text: The obligations referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to an enterprise or an organisation employing fewer than 250 persons unless the processing it carries out is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects, the processing is not occasional, or the processing includes special categories of data as referred to in Article 9 or personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences referred to in Article 10.

rules.entity: certification bodies with appropriate level of expertise in data protection
rules.action: issue and renew certification
rules.condition: after informing the supervisory authority to allow it to exercise its powers pursuant to Article 58(2) where necessary
rules.raw_text: Without prejudice to the tasks and powers of the competent supervisory authority under Articles 57 and 58, certification bodies which have an appropriate level of expertise in relation to data protection shall, after informing the supervisory authority in order to allow it to exercise its powers pursuant to point (h) of Article 58(2) where necessary, issue and renew certification.

rules.action: ensure that those certification bodies are accredited by
rules.condition: the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56; the national accreditation body named in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council in accordance with EN-ISO/IEC 17065/2012 and with the additional requirements established by the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56.
rules.raw_text: 2 Member States shall ensure that those certification bodies are accredited by one or both of the following:

rules.entity: Certification bodies referred to in paragraph 1
rules.action: be accredited only where they have:
rules.condition: demonstrated their independence and expertise in relation to the subject-matter of the certification to the satisfaction of the competent supervisory authority; undertaken to respect the criteria referred to in Article 42(5) and approved by the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56 or by the Board pursuant to Article 63; established procedures for the issuing, periodic review and withdrawal of data protection certification, seals and marks; established procedures and structures to handle complaints about infringements of the certification or the manner in which the certification has been, or is being, implemented by the controller or processor, and to make those procedures and structures transparent to data subjects and the public; and demonstrated, to the satisfaction of the competent supervisory authority, that their tasks and duties do not result in a conflict of interests.
rules.scope: Certification bodies referred to in paragraph 1
rules.raw_text: Certification bodies referred to in paragraph 1 shall be accredited in accordance with that paragraph only where they have:

rules.entity: The accreditation of certification bodies as referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article
rules.action: take place on the basis of requirements approved by
rules.condition: the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56 or by the Board pursuant to Article 63.
rules.scope: The accreditation of certification bodies as referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article
rules.raw_text: 1 The accreditation of certification bodies as referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article shall take place on the basis of requirements approved by the supervisory authority which is competent pursuant to Article 55 or 56 or by the Board pursuant to Article 63.

rules.entity: In the case of accreditation pursuant to point (b) of paragraph 1 of this Article
rules.action: those requirements shall complement those envisaged in Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 and the technical rules that describe the methods and procedures of the certification bodies.
rules.scope: In the case of accreditation pursuant to point (b) of paragraph 1 of this Article
rules.raw_text: 2 In the case of accreditation pursuant to point (b) of paragraph 1 of this Article, those requirements shall complement those envisaged in Regulation (EC) No 765/2008 and the technical rules that describe the methods and procedures of the certification bodies.

rules.entity: The certification bodies referred to in paragraph 1
rules.action: be responsible for the proper assessment leading to the certification or the withdrawal of such certification without prejudice to the responsibility of the controller or processor for compliance with this Regulation.
rules.scope: The certification bodies referred to in paragraph 1
rules.raw_text: 1 The certification bodies referred to in paragraph 1 shall be responsible for the proper assessment leading to the certification or the withdrawal of such certification without prejudice to the responsibility of the controller or processor for compliance with this Regulation.

rules.entity: The accreditation shall be issued for a maximum period of five years and may be renewed on the same conditions provided that the certification body meets the requirements set out in this Article.
rules.raw_text: 2 The accreditation shall be issued for a maximum period of five years and may be renewed on the same conditions provided that the certification body meets the requirements set out in this Article.

rules.action: transfer personal data to a third country or an international organisation
rules.condition: in the absence of a decision pursuant to Article 45(3) and if appropriate safeguards are provided and enforceable data subject rights and effective legal remedies for data subjects are available
rules.raw_text: In the absence of a decision pursuant to Article 45(3), a controller or processor may transfer personal data to a third country or an international organisation only if the controller or processor has provided appropriate safeguards, and on condition that enforceable data subject rights and effective legal remedies for data subjects are available.

rules.action: provide appropriate safeguards
rules.condition: by a legally binding and enforceable instrument between public authorities or bodies, binding corporate rules in accordance with Article 47, standard data protection clauses adopted by the Commission, standard data protection clauses adopted by a supervisory authority and approved by the Commission, an approved code of conduct pursuant to Article 40 together with binding and enforceable commitments of the controller or processor, or an approved certification mechanism pursuant to Article 42
rules.raw_text: The appropriate safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 may be provided for, without requiring any specific authorisation from a supervisory authority, by:

rules.action: provide appropriate safeguards
rules.condition: by contractual clauses between the controller or processor and the controller, processor or recipient of the personal data in the third country or international organisation or provisions to be inserted into administrative arrangements between public authorities or bodies which include enforceable and effective data subject rights
rules.raw_text: Subject to the authorisation from the competent supervisory authority, the appropriate safeguards referred to in paragraph 1 may also be provided for, in particular, by:

rules.action: act with complete independence in performing its tasks and exercising its powers
rules.condition: in accordance with this Regulation
rules.scope: performing tasks and exercising powers as a supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: Each supervisory authority shall act with complete independence in performing its tasks and exercising its powers in accordance with this Regulation.

rules.entity: Member or members of each supervisory authority
rules.action: remain free from external influence
rules.condition: whether direct or indirect and shall neither seek nor take instructions from anybody
rules.scope: in the performance of their tasks and exercise of their powers in accordance with this Regulation
rules.raw_text: The member or members of each supervisory authority shall, in the performance of their tasks and exercise of their powers in accordance with this Regulation, remain free from external influence, whether direct or indirect, and shall neither seek nor take instructions from anybody.

rules.entity: Member or members of each supervisory authority
rules.action: refrain from any action incompatible with their duties and shall not, during their term of office, engage in any incompatible occupation
rules.condition: whether gainful or not
rules.scope: during their term of office as a supervisory authority member
rules.raw_text: Member or members of each supervisory authority shall refrain from any action incompatible with their duties and shall not, during their term of office, engage in any incompatible occupation, whether gainful or not.

rules.action: ensure that each supervisory authority is provided with the human, technical and financial resources, premises and infrastructure necessary for the effective performance of its tasks and exercise of its powers
rules.condition: including those to be carried out in the context of mutual assistance, cooperation and participation in the Board
rules.scope: Each Member State regarding each supervisory authority within their jurisdiction
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is provided with the human, technical and financial resources, premises and infrastructure necessary for the effective performance of its tasks and exercise of its powers, including those to be carried out in the context of mutual assistance, cooperation and participation in the Board.

rules.action: ensure that each supervisory authority chooses and has its own staff which shall be subject to the exclusive direction of the member or members of the supervisory authority concerned
rules.condition: N/A
rules.scope: Each Member State regarding each supervisory authority within their jurisdiction
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority chooses and has its own staff which shall be subject to the exclusive direction of the member or members of the supervisory authority concerned.

rules.action: ensure that each supervisory authority is subject to financial control which does not affect its independence and that it has separate, public annual budgets
rules.condition: which may be part of the overall state or national budget
rules.scope: Each Member State regarding each supervisory authority within their jurisdiction
rules.raw_text: Each Member State shall ensure that each supervisory authority is subject to financial control which does not affect its independence and that it has separate, public annual budgets, which may be part of the overall state or national budget.

rules.action: appoint each member
rules.condition: by means of a transparent procedure by their parliament, government, head of State, or an independent body entrusted with the appointment under Member State law
rules.raw_text: Member States shall provide for each member of their supervisory authorities to be appointed by means of a transparent procedure by: – their parliament; – their government; – their head of State; or – an independent body entrusted with the appointment under Member State law.

rules.action: have the qualifications, experience and skills
rules.condition: in particular in the area of the protection of personal data
rules.raw_text: Each member shall have the qualifications, experience and skills, in particular in the area of the protection of personal data, required to perform its duties and exercise its powers.

rules.action: end
rules.condition: in the event of the expiry of the term of office, resignation or compulsory retirement
rules.raw_text: The duties of a member shall end in the event of the expiry of the term of office, resignation or compulsory retirement, in accordance with the law of the Member State concerned.

rules.action: be dismissed
rules.condition: only in cases of serious misconduct or if the member no longer fulfils the conditions required for the performance of the duties
rules.raw_text: A member shall be dismissed only in cases of serious misconduct or if the member no longer fulfils the conditions required for the performance of the duties.

rules.action: establish each supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: the establishment of each supervisory authority;

rules.action: define qualifications and eligibility conditions required to be appointed as member of each supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: the qualifications and eligibility conditions required to be appointed as member of each supervisory authority;

rules.action: define rules and procedures for the appointment of the member or members of each supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: the rules and procedures for the appointment of the member or members of each supervisory authority;

rules.action: set duration of term of the member or members of each supervisory authority to no less than four years
rules.raw_text: the duration of the term of the member or members of each supervisory authority of no less than four years,

rules.action: define eligibility for reappointment of the member or members of each supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: whether and, if so, for how many terms the member or members of each supervisory authority is eligible for reappointment;

rules.action: define conditions governing obligations, prohibitions, incompatible actions and benefits of the member or members and staff of each supervisory authority during and after term of office
rules.raw_text: the conditions governing the obligations of the member or members and staff of each supervisory authority,

rules.entity: Member or members and staff of each supervisory authority
rules.action: be subject to a duty of professional secrecy both during and after their term of office
rules.condition: with regard to any confidential information which has come to their knowledge in the course of the performance of their tasks or exercise of their powers
rules.scope: Member or members and staff of each supervisory authority
rules.raw_text: The member or members and the staff of each supervisory authority shall, in accordance with Union or Member State law,

rules.entity: Member or members and staff during their term of office
rules.action: apply duty of professional secrecy to reporting by natural persons of infringements of this Regulation
rules.scope: Member or members and staff during their term of office
rules.raw_text: during their term of office, that duty of professional secrecy shall in particular apply to reporting by natural persons of infringements of this Regulation.

rules.action: perform tasks and exercise powers in accordance with this Regulation on the territory of its own Member State
rules.condition: none
rules.scope: all cases assigned to and conferred under GDPR
rules.raw_text: Each supervisory authority shall be competent for the performance of the tasks assigned to and the exercise of the powers conferred on it in accordance with this Regulation on the territory of its own Member State.

rules.entity: supervisory authority when processing is carried out by public authorities or private bodies acting on the basis of point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1)
rules.action: be competent
rules.condition: processing is carried out by public authorities or private bodies acting on the basis of point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1), and processing is within the Member State concerned
rules.scope: within the scope of the specified conditions
rules.raw_text: Where processing is carried out by public authorities or private bodies acting on the basis of point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1), the supervisory authority of the Member State concerned shall be competent.

rules.action: not apply
rules.condition: processing is carried out by public authorities or private bodies acting on the basis of point (c) or (e) of Article 6(1)
rules.scope: within the scope of the specified conditions
rules.raw_text: In such cases Article 56 does not apply.

rules.action: not be competent to supervise
rules.condition: processing operations of courts acting in their judicial capacity
rules.scope: all cases where processing operations of courts are carried out in their judicial capacity
rules.raw_text: Supervisory authorities shall not be competent to supervise processing operations of courts acting in their judicial capacity.

rules.entity: supervisory authority of the main establishment or single establishment of the controller or processor
rules.action: act as lead supervisory authority for cross-border processing
rules.condition: carried out by that controller or processor in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60
rules.raw_text: The supervisory authority of the main establishment or of the single establishment of the controller or processor shall be competent to act as lead supervisory authority for the cross-border processing carried out by that controller or processor in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60.

rules.action: handle a complaint or possible infringement
rules.condition: if the subject matter relates only to an establishment in its Member State or substantially affects data subjects only in its Member State
rules.raw_text: By derogation from paragraph 1, each supervisory authority shall be competent to handle a complaint lodged with it or a possible infringement of this Regulation, if the subject matter relates only to an establishment in its Member State or substantially affects data subjects only in its Member State.

rules.action: decide whether to handle the case
rules.condition: within a period of three weeks after being informed and considering whether there is an establishment of the controller or processor in the Member State of which the supervisory authority informed it
rules.raw_text: Within a period of three weeks after being informed the lead supervisory authority shall decide whether or not it will handle the case in accordance with the procedure provided in Article 60,

rules.action: be the sole interlocutor of the controller or processor for cross-border processing
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority shall be the sole interlocutor of the controller or processor for the cross-border processing carried out by that controller or processor.

rules.action: monitor and enforce the application of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: monitor and enforce the application of this Regulation

rules.action: promote public awareness
rules.condition: addressing specifically to children shall receive specific attention
rules.raw_text: promote public awareness and understanding of the risks, rules, safeguards and rights in relation to processing. Activities addressed specifically to children shall receive specific attention

rules.action: advise on legislative and administrative measures relating to the protection of natural persons’ rights
rules.scope: in accordance with Member State law
rules.raw_text: advise, in accordance with Member State law, the national parliament, the government, and other institutions and bodies on legislative and administrative measures relating to the protection of natural persons’ rights and freedoms with regard to processing

rules.action: provide information to any data subject concerning the exercise of their rights under this Regulation
rules.raw_text: upon request, provide information to any data subject concerning the exercise of their rights under this Regulation

rules.action: handle complaints lodged by a data subject or by a body, organisation or association
rules.condition: in accordance with Article 80
rules.raw_text: handle complaints lodged by a data subject, or by a body, organisation or association in accordance with Article 80

rules.action: cooperate with other supervisory authorities
rules.condition: with a view to ensuring the consistency of application and enforcement of this Regulation
rules.raw_text: cooperate with, including sharing information and provide mutual assistance to, other supervisory authorities with a view to ensuring the consistency of application and enforcement of this Regulation

rules.action: conduct investigations on the application of this Regulation
rules.condition: on the basis of information received from another supervisory authority or other public authority
rules.raw_text: conduct investigations on the application of this Regulation, including on the basis of information received from another supervisory authority or other public authority

rules.action: monitor relevant developments
rules.condition: insofar as they have an impact on the protection of personal data, in particular the development of information and communication technologies and commercial practices
rules.raw_text: monitor relevant developments, insofar as they have an impact on the protection of personal data, in particular the development of information and communication technologies and commercial practices

rules.action: adopt standard contractual clauses
rules.raw_text: adopt standard contractual clauses referred to in Article 28 (8) and in point (d) of Article 46 (2)

rules.action: establish and maintain a list
rules.condition: in relation to the requirement for data protection impact assessment pursuant to Article 35 (4)
rules.raw_text: establish and maintain a list in relation to the requirement for data protection impact assessment pursuant to Article 35 (4)

rules.action: give advice on the processing operations
rules.condition: referred to in Article 36 (2)
rules.raw_text: give advice on the processing operations referred to in Article 36 (2)

rules.action: encourage the drawing up of codes of conduct
rules.raw_text: encourage the drawing up of codes of conduct pursuant to Article 40 (1)

rules.action: provide an opinion and approve codes of conduct
rules.condition: which provide sufficient safeguards
rules.raw_text: approve such codes of conduct which provide sufficient safeguards, pursuant to Article 40 (5)

rules.action: encourage the establishment of data protection certification mechanisms and of data protection seals and marks
rules.raw_text: encourage the establishment of data protection certification mechanisms and of data protection seals and marks pursuant to Article 42 (1)

rules.action: approve the criteria of certification
rules.raw_text: approve the criteria of certification pursuant to Article 42 (5)

rules.action: carry out a periodic review of certifications
rules.raw_text: carry out a periodic review of certifications issued in accordance with Article 42 (7)

rules.action: draft and publish the requirements for accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct
rules.raw_text: draft and publish the requirements for accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct pursuant to Article 41

rules.action: conduct the accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct
rules.condition: pursuant to Article 41
rules.raw_text: conduct the accreditation of a body for monitoring codes of conduct pursuant to Article 41

rules.action: draft and publish the requirements for accreditation of a certification body
rules.raw_text: draft and publish the requirements for accreditation of a certification body pursuant to Article 43

rules.action: order the controller and processor to provide any information
rules.condition: for the performance of its tasks
rules.raw_text: to order the controller and the processor, and, where applicable, the controller’s or the processor’s representative to provide any information it requires for the performance of its tasks;

rules.action: carry out investigations in the form of data protection audits
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to carry out investigations in the form of data protection audits;

rules.action: carry out a review on certifications issued pursuant to Article 42(7)
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to carry out a review on certifications issued pursuant to  Article 42(7);

rules.action: notify the controller or processor of an alleged infringement of this Regulation
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to notify the controller or the processor of an alleged infringement of this Regulation;

rules.action: obtain access to all personal data and to all information necessary for the performance of its tasks
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to obtain, from the controller and the processor, access to all personal data and to all information necessary for the performance of its tasks;

rules.action: obtain access to any premises of the controller and the processor
rules.condition: including to any data processing equipment and means, in accordance with Union or Member State procedural law
rules.raw_text: to obtain access to any premises of the controller and the processor, including to any data processing equipment and means, in accordance with Union or Member State procedural law.

rules.action: issue warnings to a controller or processor that intended processing operations are likely to infringe provisions of this Regulation
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to issue warnings to a controller or processor that intended processing operations are likely to infringe provisions of this Regulation;

rules.action: issue reprimands to a controller or processor where processing operations have infringed provisions of this Regulation
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to issue reprimands to a controller or a processor where processing operations have infringed provisions of this Regulation;

rules.action: order the controller or the processor to comply with the data subject’s requests to exercise his or her rights pursuant to this Regulation
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to order the controller or the processor to comply with the data subject’s requests to exercise his or her rights pursuant to this Regulation;

rules.action: order the controller or processor to bring processing operations into compliance with the provisions of this Regulation
rules.condition: where appropriate, in a specified manner and within a specified period
rules.raw_text: to order the controller or processor to bring processing operations into compliance with the provisions of this Regulation, where appropriate, in a specified manner and within a specified period;

rules.action: order the controller to communicate a personal data breach to the data subject
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to order the controller to communicate a personal data breach to the data subject;

rules.action: impose a temporary or definitive limitation including a ban on processing
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to impose a temporary or definitive limitation including a ban on processing;

rules.action: order the rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing pursuant to Articles 16, 17 and 18
rules.condition: and the notification of such actions to recipients to whom the personal data have been disclosed pursuant to Article 17(2) and Article 19
rules.raw_text: to order the rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing pursuant to  Articles 16, 17 and 18 and the notification of such actions to recipients to whom the personal data have been disclosed pursuant to Article 17(2) and Article 19;

rules.action: withdraw a certification or order the certification body to withdraw a certification issued pursuant to Articles 42 and 43
rules.condition: or to order the certification body not to issue certification if the requirements for the certification are not or are no longer met
rules.raw_text: to withdraw a certification or to order the certification body to withdraw a certification issued pursuant to  Articles 42 and 43, or to order the certification body not to issue certification if the requirements for the certification are not or are no longer met;

rules.action: impose an administrative fine pursuant to Article 83
rules.condition: in addition to, or instead of measures referred to in this paragraph, depending on the circumstances of each individual case
rules.raw_text: to impose an administrative fine pursuant to  Article 83, in addition to, or instead of measures referred to in this paragraph, depending on the circumstances of each individual case;

rules.action: order the suspension of data flows to a recipient in a third country or to an international organisation
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to order the suspension of data flows to a recipient in a third country or to an international organisation.

rules.action: advise the controller in accordance with the prior consultation procedure referred to in Article 36
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to advise the controller in accordance with the prior consultation procedure referred to in  Article 36;

rules.action: issue, on its own initiative or on request, opinions to the national parliament, the Member State government or, in accordance with Member State law, to other institutions and bodies as well as to the public on any issue related to the protection of personal data
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to issue, on its own initiative or on request, opinions to the national parliament, the Member State government or, in accordance with Member State law, to other institutions and bodies as well as to the public on any issue related to the protection of personal data;

rules.action: authorise
rules.condition: unspecified
rules.raw_text: to authorise

rules.action: draws up an annual report on its activities
rules.scope: includes a list of types of infringement notified and types of measures taken in accordance with Art. 58 (2)
rules.raw_text: Each supervisory authority shall draw up an annual report on its activities, which may include a list of types of infringement notified and types of measures taken in accordance with Article 58 (2).

rules.action: are transmitted to the national parliament, the government and other authorities as designated by Member State law.
rules.scope: No specified scope. Presumably refers to transmission of the reports.
rules.raw_text: Those reports shall be transmitted to the national parliament, the government and other authorities as designated by Member State law.

rules.action: are made available to the public, to the Commission and to the Board.
rules.scope: No specified scope. Presumably refers to making the reports publicly available.
rules.raw_text: They shall be made available to the public, to the Commission and to the Board.

rules.action: cooperate with other supervisory authorities concerned
rules.condition: in accordance with this Article in an endeavour to reach consensus
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority shall cooperate with the other supervisory authorities concerned in accordance with this Article in an endeavour to reach consensus.

rules.entity: lead supervisory authority, supervisory authorities concerned
rules.action: exchange all relevant information with each other
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall exchange all relevant information with each other.

rules.action: request mutual assistance, conduct joint operations
rules.condition: pursuant to Article 61 or 62
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority may request at any time other supervisory authorities concerned to provide mutual assistance pursuant to Article 61 and may conduct joint operations pursuant to Article 62, in particular for carrying out investigations or for monitoring the implementation of a measure concerning a controller or processor established in another Member State.

rules.action: communicate relevant information
rules.raw_text: The lead supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate the relevant information on the matter to the other supervisory authorities concerned.

rules.action: submit draft decision for opinion, take due account of views
rules.raw_text: It shall without delay submit a draft decision to the other supervisory authorities concerned for their opinion and take due account of their views.

rules.action: express a relevant and reasoned objection to draft decision
rules.condition: within a period of four weeks after having been consulted in accordance with paragraph 3 of this Article
rules.raw_text: Where any of the other supervisory authorities concerned within a period of four weeks after having been consulted in accordance with paragraph 3 of this Article, expresses a relevant and reasoned objection to the draft decision, the lead supervisory authority shall, if it does not follow the relevant and reasoned objection or is of the opinion that the objection is not relevant or reasoned, submit the matter to the consistency mechanism referred to in Article 63.

rules.action: submit revised draft decision for opinion
rules.condition: Where the lead supervisory authority intends to follow the relevant and reasoned objection made
rules.raw_text: Where the lead supervisory authority intends to follow the relevant and reasoned objection made, it shall submit to the other supervisory authorities concerned a revised draft decision for their opinion.

rules.entity: lead supervisory authority, supervisory authorities concerned
rules.action: be deemed to be in agreement with that draft decision and shall be bound by it
rules.condition: Where none of the other supervisory authorities concerned has objected to the draft decision submitted by the lead supervisory authority within the period referred to in paragraphs 4 and 5
rules.raw_text: Where none of the other supervisory authorities concerned has objected to the draft decision submitted by the lead supervisory authority within the period referred to in paragraphs 4 and 5, the lead supervisory authority and the supervisory authorities concerned shall be deemed to be in agreement with that draft decision and shall be bound by it.

rules.action: adopt and notify decision
