rules.raw_text: In case of a particular risk of a breach of the security of the network, the provider of a publicly available electronic communications service must inform the subscribers concerning such risk and, where the risk lies outside the scope of the measures to be taken by the service provider, of any possible remedies, including an indication of the likely costs involved.

rules.entity: provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: notify the competent national authority and subscribers of a personal data breach
rules.condition: when a personal data breach occurs
rules.scope: competent national authority and subscribers concerned
rules.raw_text: 3.   In the case of a personal data breach, the provider of publicly available electronic communications services shall, without undue delay, notify the personal data breach to the competent national authority.

rules.entity: provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: notify the subscriber or individual of a personal data breach
rules.condition: when the personal data breach is likely to adversely affect the personal data or privacy of a subscriber or individual
rules.raw_text: 3.   In the case of a personal data breach, the provider of publicly available electronic communications services shall, without undue delay, notify the personal data breach to the competent national authority. When the personal data breach is likely to adversely affect the personal data or privacy of a subscriber or individual, the provider shall also notify the subscriber or individual of the breach without undue delay.

rules.entity: provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: implement appropriate technological protection measures
rules.condition: to be exempt from notifying a subscriber or individual of the personal data breach
rules.scope: personal data breach notification to a subscriber or individual concerned
rules.raw_text: Notification of a personal data breach to a subscriber or individual concerned shall not be required if the provider has demonstrated to the satisfaction of the competent authority that it has implemented appropriate technological protection measures, and that those measures were applied to the data concerned by the security breach. Such technological protection measures shall render the data unintelligible to any person who is not authorised to access it.

rules.action: adopt technical implementing measures
rules.condition: concerning the circumstances, format and procedures applicable to the information and notification requirements referred to in this Article
rules.raw_text: Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC and the European Data Protection Supervisor, adopt technical implementing measures concerning the circumstances, format and procedures applicable to the information and notification requirements referred to in this Article.

rules.action: shall involve all relevant stakeholders
rules.condition: in order to be informed of the best available technical and economic means of implementation of this Article.
rules.raw_text: Those measures, designed to amend non-essential elements of this Directive by supplementing it, shall be adopted in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14a(2).

rules.action: ensure confidentiality of communications and the related traffic data
rules.condition: through national legislation
rules.scope: public communications network and publicly available electronic communications services
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure the confidentiality of communications and the related traffic data by means of a public communications network and publicly available electronic communications services, through national legislation.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: the subscriber or user has given his or her consent, having been provided with clear and comprehensive information in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC about the purposes of the processing
rules.scope: storing of information or gaining access to information already stored in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States shall ensure that the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent,

rules.action: not prevent
rules.condition: technical storage or access for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network, or as strictly necessary in order for the provider of an information society service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user to provide the service
rules.scope: storing of information or gaining access to information already stored in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user
rules.raw_text: 3.   ...this shall not prevent any technical storage or access...

rules.entity: provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: must erase or make anonymous
rules.condition: when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication
rules.scope: traffic data relating to subscribers and users
rules.raw_text: Traffic data relating to subscribers and users processed and stored by the provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service must be erased or made anonymous when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States shall ensure that the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent,

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: the provision of clear and comprehensive information, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, about the purposes of the processing
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States shall ensure that the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent, having been provided with clear and comprehensive information, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC,

rules.action: not prevent
rules.condition: any technical storage or access for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network, or as strictly necessary in order for the provider of an information society service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user to provide the service
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States shall ensure that the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent, having been provided with clear and comprehensive information, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, unless it is necessary for technical storage or access for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network, or as strictly necessary in order for the provider of an information society service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user to provide the service.

rules.action: receive non-itemised bills
rules.raw_text: 1. Subscribers shall have the right to receive non-itemised bills.

rules.action: apply national provisions
rules.condition: to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the right to privacy of calling users and called subscribers
rules.raw_text: 2. Member States shall apply national provisions in order to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the right to privacy of calling users and called subscribers,

rules.action: offer the possibility of preventing presentation of calling line identification on a per-call basis to the calling user, free of charge
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the calling user the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing presentation of calling line identification of incoming calls, free of charge for reasonable use
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge for reasonable use of this function, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification of incoming calls.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of rejecting incoming calls where calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered and when the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered and where the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means, of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing the presentation of connected line identification to the calling user, free of charge
rules.condition: when presentation of connected line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: Where presentation of connected line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the connected line identification to the calling user.

rules.action: apply paragraph 1 to calls to third countries originating in the Community
rules.scope: to calls to third countries originating in the Community
rules.raw_text: Paragraph 1 shall also apply with regard to calls to third countries originating in the Community.

rules.entity: service provider of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that where presentation of calling and/or connected line identification is offered, the providers of publicly available electronic communications services inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4.

rules.action: process location data other than traffic data
rules.condition: when made anonymous or with the consent of users/subscribers for the provision of a value added service
rules.scope: public communications networks or publicly available electronic communications services
rules.raw_text: such data may only be processed when they are made anonymous, or with the consent of the users or subscribers to the extent and for the duration necessary for the provision of a value added service.

rules.action: be informed prior to obtaining consent, withdraw consent at any time
rules.condition: none
rules.scope: location data other than traffic data
rules.raw_text: The service provider must inform the users or subscribers, prior to obtaining their consent, of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value added service.

rules.action: refuse processing of location data other than traffic data temporarily
rules.condition: none
rules.scope: each connection to the network or for each transmission of a communication
rules.raw_text: Where consent of the users or subscribers has been obtained for the processing of location data other than traffic data, the user or subscriber must continue to have the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of temporarily refusing the processing of such data for each connection to the network or for each transmission of a communication.

rules.entity: persons processing location data other than traffic data
rules.action: be restricted to persons acting under the authority of the provider and necessary for providing value added service
rules.condition: none
rules.scope: processing in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2
rules.raw_text: Processing of location data other than traffic data in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2 must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of the provider of the public communications network or publicly available communications service or of the third party providing the value added service, and must be restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of providing the value added service.

rules.entity: provider of a public communications network and/or publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: override the elimination of calling line identification
rules.condition: upon application of a subscriber requesting the tracing of malicious or nuisance calls
rules.scope: under transparent procedures according to national law, with stored and made available data containing the identification of the calling subscriber
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that there are transparent procedures governing the way in which a provider of a public communications network and/or a publicly available electronic communications service may override: (a) ...

rules.entity: provider of a public communications network and/or publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: temporary denial or absence of consent for the processing of location data
rules.condition: for organisations dealing with emergency calls and recognized as such by a Member State, including law enforcement agencies, ambulance services, and fire brigades
rules.scope: on a per-line basis for the purpose of responding to such calls
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that there are transparent procedures governing the way in which a provider of a public communications network and/or publicly available electronic communications service may override: (b) ...

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: any subscriber has the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of stopping automatic call forwarding by a third party to the subscriber's terminal.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that any subscriber has the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of stopping automatic call forwarding by a third party to the subscriber's terminal.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: subscribers are informed about the purpose of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers and any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory, before they are included in the directory.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about the purpose(s) of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers available to the public or obtainable through directory enquiry services, in which their personal data can be included and of any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: subscribers are given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, verify, correct or withdraw such data, and not being included, verifying, correcting or withdrawing personal data from it shall be free of charge.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that subscribers are given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, and if so, which, to the extent that such data are relevant for the purpose of the directory as determined by the provider of the directory, and to verify, correct or withdraw such data. Not being included in a public subscriber directory, verifying, correcting or withdrawing personal data from it shall be free of charge.

rules.action: may require
rules.condition: additional consent be asked of the subscribers for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers.
rules.raw_text: Member States may require that for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers, additional consent be asked of the subscribers.

rules.action: ensure
rules.condition: the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall also ensure, in the framework of Community law and applicable national legislation, that the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.

rules.entity: automatic calling machines, facsimile machines (fax) or electronic mail
rules.action: use for direct marketing may be allowed only in respect of subscribers or users who have given their prior consent.
rules.raw_text: The use of automated calling and communication systems without human intervention (automatic calling machines), facsimile machines (fax) or electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing may be allowed only in respect of subscribers or users who have given their prior consent.

rules.entity: same natural or legal person as obtained electronic contact details for electronic mail
rules.action: use these electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services
rules.condition: customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object, free of charge and in an easy manner, to such use of electronic contact details at the time of their collection and on the occasion of each message in case the customer has not initially refused such use.
rules.raw_text: Notwithstanding paragraph 1, where a natural or legal person obtains from its customers their electronic contact details for electronic mail, in the context of the sale of a product or a service, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, the same natural or legal person may use these electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services provided that customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object, free of charge and in an easy manner, to such use of electronic contact details at the time of their collection and on the occasion of each message in case the customer has not initially refused such use.

rules.action: take appropriate measures to ensure that unsolicited communications for the purposes of direct marketing, in cases other than those referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, are not allowed either without the consent of the subscribers or users concerned or in respect of subscribers or users who do not wish to receive these communications
rules.condition: the choice between these options to be determined by national legislation, taking into account that both options must be free of charge for the subscriber or user.
rules.raw_text: Member States shall take appropriate measures to ensure that unsolicited communications for the purposes of direct marketing, in cases other than those referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, are not allowed either without the consent of the subscribers or users concerned or in respect of subscribers or users who do not wish to receive these communications, the choice between these options to be determined by national legislation, taking into account that both options must be free of charge for the subscriber or user.

rules.entity: sending electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing
rules.action: which disguise or conceal the identity of the sender on whose behalf the communication is made, which contravene
rules.raw_text: In any event, the practice of sending electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing which disguise or conceal the identity of the sender on whose behalf the communication is made, which contravene

rules.entity: provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: processed and stored traffic data relating to subscribers and users
rules.condition: when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication
rules.scope: traffic data must be erased or made anonymous
rules.raw_text: Traffic data relating to subscribers and users processed and stored by the provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service must be erased or made anonymous when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication

rules.action: ensure no mandatory requirements for specific technical features are imposed on terminal or other electronic communication equipment
rules.condition: subject to paragraphs 2 and 3
rules.scope: implementing the provisions of this Directive
rules.raw_text: In implementing the provisions of this Directive, Member States shall ensure, subject to paragraphs 2 and 3, that no mandatory requirements for specific technical features are imposed on terminal or other electronic communication equipment which could impede the placing of equipment on the market and the free circulation of such equipment in and between Member States.

rules.action: inform the Commission in accordance with the procedure provided for by Directive 98/34/EC
rules.condition: where provisions of this Directive can be implemented only by requiring specific technical features in electronic communications networks
rules.scope: implementing the provisions of this Directive
rules.raw_text: Where provisions of this Directive can be implemented only by requiring specific technical features in electronic communications networks, Member States shall inform the Commission in accordance with the procedure provided for by Directive 98/34/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 22 June 1998 laying down a procedure for the provision of information in the field of technical standards and regulations and of rules on information society services ( 9 ).

rules.action: adopt measures to ensure that terminal equipment is constructed in a way that is compatible with the right of users to protect and control the use of their personal data
rules.condition: where required
rules.scope: in accordance with Directive 1999/5/EC and Council Decision 87/95/EEC of 22 December 1986 on standardisation in the field of information technology and communications ( 10 )
rules.raw_text: Where required, measures may be adopted to ensure that terminal equipment is constructed in a way that is compatible with the right of users to protect and control the use of their personal data, in accordance with Directive 1999/5/EC and Council Decision 87/95/EEC of 22 December 1986 on standardisation in the field of information technology and communications ( 10 ).

rules.action: shall lay down the appropriate national rules to ensure that the processing of personal data in the course of a communication by electronic means does not infringe this Directive
rules.raw_text: 2.   Member States shall lay down the appropriate national rules to ensure that the processing of personal data in the course of a communication by electronic means does not infringe this Directive.

rules.action: have the right to receive non-itemised bills.
rules.raw_text: Subscribers shall have the right to receive non-itemised bills.

rules.action: apply national provisions
rules.condition: to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the right to privacy of calling users and called subscribers
rules.raw_text: Member States shall apply national provisions in order to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the right to privacy of calling users and called subscribers

rules.action: offer the possibility of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis to the calling user (free of charge)
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 1.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the calling user the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification of incoming calls (free of charge for reasonable use)
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 2.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge for reasonable use of this function, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification of incoming calls.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered and when the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established
rules.raw_text: 3.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered and where the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means, of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing the presentation of the connected line identification to the calling user (free of charge)
rules.condition: when presentation of connected line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 4.   Where presentation of connected line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the connected line identification to the calling user.

rules.action: apply regarding calls to third countries originating in the Community
rules.raw_text: 5.   Paragraph 1 shall also apply with regard to calls to third countries originating in the Community.

rules.action: ensure that where presentation of calling and/or connected line identification is offered, providers inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4
rules.raw_text: 6.   Member States shall ensure that where presentation of calling and/or connected line identification is offered, the providers of publicly available electronic communications services inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4.

rules.action: receive non-itemised bills
rules.raw_text: Subscribers shall have the right to receive non-itemised bills.

rules.action: apply national provisions
rules.condition: to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the right to privacy of calling users and called subscribers
rules.raw_text: Member States shall apply national provisions in order to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the right to privacy of calling users and called subscribers, for example by ensuring that sufficient alternative privacy enhancing methods of communications or payments are available to such users and subscribers.

rules.action: offer the possibility of preventing the presentation of calling line identification on a per-call basis for the calling user (per-call) and on a per-line basis for the calling subscriber
rules.condition: presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 1.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the calling user the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing the presentation of calling line identification for incoming calls
rules.condition: presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 2.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge for reasonable use of this function, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification of incoming calls.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber
rules.condition: presentation of calling line identification is offered and presented prior to the call being established
rules.raw_text: 3.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered and where the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means, of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility of preventing the presentation of connected line identification to the calling user
rules.condition: presentation of connected line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 4.   Where presentation of connected line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the connected line identification to the calling user.

rules.action: apply rules 1, 2, 3, and 4 also to calls to third countries originating in the Community and incoming calls originating in third countries
rules.condition: not specified
rules.scope: calls to third countries and incoming calls from third countries
rules.raw_text: 5.   Paragraph 1 shall also apply with regard to calls to third countries originating in the Community. Paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 shall also apply to incoming calls originating in third countries.

rules.entity: providers of publicly available electronic communications services
rules.action: inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4
rules.condition: presentation of calling and/or connected line identification is offered
rules.raw_text: 6.   Member States shall ensure that where presentation of calling and/or connected line identification is offered, the providers of publicly available electronic communications services inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4.

rules.action: allow storing of information or gaining access to information already stored
rules.condition: subscriber or user has given consent and provided clear and comprehensive information about the purposes of processing, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC
rules.scope: terminal equipment of a subscriber or user
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States shall ensure that the storing of information, or the gaining of access to information already stored, in the terminal equipment of a subscriber or user is only allowed on condition that the subscriber or user concerned has given his or her consent, having been provided with clear and comprehensive information, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, inter alia, about the purposes of the processing. This shall not prevent any technical storage or access for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network, or as strictly necessary in order for the provider of an information society service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user to provide the service.

rules.action: ensure the confidentiality of communications and related traffic data
rules.condition: through national legislation
rules.scope: public communications network and publicly available electronic communications services
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure the confidentiality of communications and the related traffic data by means of a public communications network and publicly available electronic communications services, through national legislation.

rules.entity: provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: processed and stored traffic data relating to subscribers and users
rules.condition: when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication
rules.raw_text: Traffic data relating to subscribers and users processed and stored by the provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service must be erased or made anonymous when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication

rules.action: offer the possibility to prevent calling line identification presentation on a per-call basis
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.scope: to the calling user, free of charge
rules.raw_text: 1.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the calling user the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility to prevent calling line identification presentation of incoming calls
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered
rules.scope: free of charge for reasonable use
rules.raw_text: 2.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge for reasonable use of this function, of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification of incoming calls.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility to reject incoming calls when calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber
rules.condition: when presentation of calling line identification is offered and where the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established
rules.raw_text: 3.   Where presentation of calling line identification is offered and where the calling line identification is presented prior to the call being established, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means, of rejecting incoming calls where the presentation of the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user or subscriber.

rules.action: offer the called subscriber the possibility to prevent connected line identification presentation to the calling user

rules.raw_text: 4.   Where presentation of connected line identification is offered, the service provider must offer the called subscriber the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of preventing the presentation of the connected line identification to the calling user.

rules.raw_text: 5.   Paragraph 1 shall also apply with regard to calls to third countries originating in the Community.

rules.raw_text: 6.   Member States shall ensure that where presentation of calling and/or connected line identification is offered, the providers of publicly available electronic communications services inform the public thereof and of the possibilities set out in paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 4.

rules.action: process location data other than traffic data
rules.condition: when made anonymous or with consent of users or subscribers
rules.scope: for the provision of a value added service
rules.raw_text: Such data may only be processed when they are made anonymous, or with the consent of the users or subscribers to the extent and for the duration necessary for the provision of a value added service.

rules.action: inform users or subscribers
rules.condition: prior to obtaining their consent
rules.scope: of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value added service.
rules.raw_text: The service provider must inform the users or subscribers, prior to obtaining their consent, of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value added service.

rules.action: withdraw their consent for the processing of location data other than traffic data
rules.condition: at any time
rules.raw_text: Users or subscribers shall be given the possibility to withdraw their consent for the processing of location data other than traffic data at any time.

rules.action: temporarily refuse the processing of such data
rules.condition: using a simple means and free of charge for each connection to the network or for each transmission of a communication
rules.raw_text: Where consent of the users or subscribers has been obtained for the processing of location data other than traffic data, the user or subscriber must continue to have the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of temporarily refusing the processing of such data for each connection to the network or for each transmission of a communication.

rules.entity: persons acting under the authority of the provider
rules.action: process location data other than traffic data
rules.condition: restricted to what is necessary for the purposes of providing the value added service
rules.raw_text: Processing of location data other than traffic data in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2 must be restricted to persons acting under the authority of the provider of the public communications network or publicly available communications service or of the third party providing the value added service,

rules.action: use automated calling and communication systems, facsimile machines (fax) or electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing
rules.condition: only in respect of subscribers or users who have given their prior consent
rules.scope: automatic calling machines, fax, and electronic mail
rules.raw_text: The use of automated calling and communication systems without human intervention (automatic calling machines), facsimile machines (fax) or electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing may be allowed only in respect of subscribers or users who have given their prior consent.

rules.action: use electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services
rules.condition: customers have given their electronic contact details in the context of the sale of a product or a service, and customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object to such use
rules.raw_text: Notwithstanding paragraph 1, where a natural or legal person obtains from its customers their electronic contact details for electronic mail, in the context of the sale of a product or a service, in accordance with Directive 95/46/EC, the same natural or legal person may use these electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services provided that customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object, free of charge and in an easy manner, to such use of electronic contact details at the time of their collection and on the occasion of each message in case the customer has not initially refused such use.

rules.action: ensure that unsolicited communications for the purposes of direct marketing are not allowed
rules.condition: in cases other than those referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, either without the consent of the subscribers or users concerned or in respect of subscribers or users who do not wish to receive these communications
rules.raw_text: Member States shall take appropriate measures to ensure that unsolicited communications for the purposes of direct marketing, in cases other than those referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2, are not allowed either without the consent of the subscribers or users concerned or in respect of subscribers or users who do not wish to receive these communications, the choice between these options to be determined by national legislation, taking into account that both options must be free of charge for the subscriber or user.

rules.action: send electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing which disguise or conceal the identity of the sender on whose behalf the communication is made, or contravene
rules.raw_text: In any event, the practice of sending electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing which disguise or conceal the identity of the sender on whose behalf the communication is made, which contravene

rules.entity: provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service
rules.action: must erase or make anonymous traffic data
rules.condition: when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication without prejudice to paragraphs 2, 3 and 5 of this Article
rules.scope: traffic data relating to subscribers and users processed and stored by the provider
rules.raw_text: Traffic data relating to subscribers and users processed and stored by the provider of a public communications network or publicly available electronic communications service must be erased or made anonymous when it is no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication without prejudice to paragraphs 2, 3 and 5 of this Article

rules.action: provide for the harmonisation of national provisions required to ensure an equivalent level of protection of fundamental rights and freedoms, and in particular the right to privacy and confidentiality, with respect to the processing of personal data in the electronic communication sector and to ensure the free movement of such data and of electronic communication equipment and services in the Community.
rules.condition: <No condition specified>
rules.scope: Within the scope of the Treaty establishing the European Community, excluding activities covered by Titles V and VI of the Treaty on European Union, and activities concerning public security, defence, State security (including the economic well-being of the State when the activities relate to State security matters) and the activities of the State in areas of criminal law.
rules.raw_text: This Directive provides for the harmonisation of the national provisions required to ensure an equivalent level of protection of fundamental rights and freedoms, and in particular the right to privacy and confidentiality, with respect to the processing of personal data in the electronic communication sector and to ensure the free movement of such data and of electronic communication equipment and services in the Community.

rules.action: particularise and complement Directive 95/46/EC for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 1.
rules.condition: <No condition specified>
rules.raw_text: The provisions of this Directive particularise and complement Directive 95/46/EC for the purposes mentioned in paragraph 1.

rules.action: provide for protection of the legitimate interests of subscribers who are legal persons.
rules.condition: <No condition specified>
rules.raw_text: This Directive shall not apply to activities which fall outside the scope of the Treaty establishing the European Community, such as those covered by Titles V and VI of the Treaty on European Union, and in any case to activities concerning public security, defence, State security (including the economic well-being of the State when the activities relate to State security matters) and the activities of the State in areas of criminal law.

rules.condition: amend non-essential elements of this Directive

rules.raw_text: Those measures, designed to amend non-essential elements of this Directive by supplementing it, shall be adopted.

rules.condition: referred to in Article 14a(2)
rules.scope: Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC and European Data Protection Supervisor
rules.raw_text: Those measures shall be adopted in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14a(2).

rules.action: lay down rules on penalties and take measures to ensure implementation
rules.condition: for infringements of the national provisions adopted pursuant to this Directive
rules.scope: national provisions adopted pursuant to this Directive
rules.raw_text: Member States shall lay down the rules on penalties, including criminal sanctions where appropriate, applicable to infringements of the national provisions adopted pursuant to this Directive and shall take all measures necessary to ensure that they are implemented.

rules.action: ensure competent national authority has power to order cessation of infringements
rules.condition: for infringements referred to in paragraph 1
rules.scope: national provisions adopted pursuant to this Directive
rules.raw_text: 2. Without prejudice to any judicial remedy which might be available, Member States shall ensure that the competent national authority and, where relevant, other national bodies have the power to order the cessation of the infringements referred to in paragraph 1.

rules.action: ensure competent national authority and other national bodies have necessary investigative powers
rules.scope: national provisions adopted pursuant to this Directive
rules.raw_text: 3. Member States shall ensure that the competent national authority and, where relevant, other national bodies have the necessary investigative powers and resources, including the power to obtain any relevant information they might need to monitor and enforce national provisions adopted pursuant to this Directive.

rules.action: adopt measures for effective cross-border cooperation in enforcement of national laws
rules.scope: national laws adopted pursuant to this Directive and cross-border data flows
rules.raw_text: 4. The relevant national regulatory authorities may adopt measures to ensure effective cross-border cooperation in the enforcement of the national laws adopted pursuant to this Directive and to create harmonised conditions for the provision of services involving cross-border data flows.

rules.action: adopt technical implementing measures
rules.condition: concerning the circumstances, format and procedures applicable to the information and notification requirements referred to in this Article.
rules.scope: information and notification requirements in this Article
rules.raw_text: Article 29 of Directive 95/46/EC and the European Data Protection Supervisor, adopt technical implementing measures concerning the circumstances, format and procedures applicable to the information and notification requirements referred to in this Article.

rules.action: involve all relevant stakeholders
rules.condition: particularly in order to be informed of the best available technical and economic means of implementation of this Article.
rules.scope: best available technical and economic means of implementation of this Article
rules.raw_text: When adopting such measures, the Commission shall involve all relevant stakeholders particularly in order to be informed of the best available technical and economic means of implementation of this Article.

rules.action: adopt measures
rules.condition: in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14a(2).
rules.scope: regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14a(2)
rules.raw_text: Those measures, designed to amend non-essential elements of this Directive by supplementing it, shall be adopted in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14a(2).

rules.raw_text: Transitional arrangements shall apply during the first 2 years following the date of application of this Regulation, regarding their processing activities subject to the obligations under this Regulation.

rules.action: ensure that any subscriber has the possibility
rules.condition: using a simple means and free of charge, of stopping automatic call forwarding by a third party to the subscriber's terminal
rules.raw_text: Member States shall ensure that any subscriber has the possibility, using a simple means and free of charge, of stopping automatic call forwarding by a third party to the subscriber's terminal.

rules.action: ensure that subscribers are informed about the purpose of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers, and given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in it, verify, correct or withdraw such data.
rules.condition: before they are included in the directory and relevant for the purpose of the directory
rules.raw_text: 1.   Member States shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before they are included in the directory, about the purpose(s) of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers available to the public or obtainable through directory enquiry services, in which their personal data can be included and of any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory. 2.   Member States shall ensure that subscribers are given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, and if so, which, to the extent that such data are relevant for the purpose of the directory as determined by the provider of the directory, and to verify, correct or withdraw such data. Not being included in a public subscriber directory, verifying, correcting or withdrawing personal data from it shall be free of charge.

rules.action: may require that for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers, additional consent be asked of the subscribers.
rules.raw_text: 3.   Member States may require that for any purpose of a public directory other than the search of contact details of persons on the basis of their name and, where necessary, a minimum of other identifiers, additional consent be asked of the subscribers.

rules.action: shall also ensure, in the framework of Community law and applicable national legislation, that the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.
rules.raw_text: 4.   Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall apply to subscribers who are natural persons. Member States shall also ensure, in the framework of Community law and applicable national legislation, that the legitimate interests of subscribers other than natural persons with regard to their entry in public directories are sufficiently protected.
